annotations.label: mobility
annotations.definition: A quality of inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to move freely.
annotations.comment: Should be defined using translocation.

annotations.label: speed
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's scalar absolute value of the rate of change of the bearer's position.

annotations.label: age
annotations.definition: A time quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of how long the bearer has existed.

annotations.label: color
annotations.definition: A composite chromatic quality composed of hue, saturation and intensity parts.

annotations.label: color hue
annotations.definition: A chromatic scalar-circular quality inhering in an object that manifests in an observer by virtue of the dominant wavelength of the visible light; may be subject to fiat divisions, typically into 7 or 8 spectra.

annotations.label: color brightness
annotations.definition: A scalar optical property that is the intensity, value or amount of perceived light.
annotations.comment: Color brightness refers to the intensity, lightness or value of the light present. Think of this as a dimmer switch.

annotations.label: color saturation
annotations.definition: A scalar chromatic property that is the degree of purity of perceived light.
annotations.comment: Color saturation refers to the amount of white light or gray paint mixed in with the hue (single wavelength) and is a measure of color purity.

annotations.label: fluorescence
annotations.definition: A luminous flux quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's emitting longer wavelength light following the absorption of shorter wavelength radiation; fluorescence is common with aromatic compounds with several rings joined together.

annotations.label: color pattern
annotations.definition: A chromatic property that is the relative position of different hues or degrees of saturation.

annotations.label: compatibility
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to harmonious coexistence.
annotations.comment: Needs redefined or obsoleted. Is this the same as genetic incompatibility? Moved to organismal quality.

annotations.label: composition
annotations.definition: A single physical entity inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's quantities or relative ratios of subparts.
annotations.comment: For example calcium composition (which may inhere in bone), haemoglobin composition (which may inhere in blood).

annotations.label: concentration of
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a substance by virtue of the amount of the bearer's there is mixed with another substance.

annotations.label: consistency
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's density, firmness, or viscosity.

annotations.label: direction
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's orientation in space.
annotations.comment: TODO: consider merging with angular placement.

annotations.label: distance
annotations.definition: A quality that is the extent of space between two entities.

annotations.label: flavor
annotations.definition: A quality of a physical entity inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer's molecules are being perceived by a taste and odorant receptors.

annotations.label: frequency
annotations.definition: A physical quality which inheres in a bearer by virtue of the number of the bearer's repetitive actions in a particular time.

annotations.label: biological sex
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to undergo sexual reproduction in order to differentiate the individuals or types involved.

annotations.label: hardness
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resistance to pressure, being broken, or pierced

annotations.label: intensity
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's possessing or displaying a distinctive feature in type or degree or effect or force.

annotations.label: life span
annotations.definition: A time quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's expected maximum age.

annotations.label: morphology
annotations.definition: A quality of a single physical entity inhering in the bearer by virtue of the bearer's size or shape or structure.

annotations.label: shape
annotations.definition: A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ratios of distances between its features (points, edges, surfaces and also holes etc).
annotations.comment: Shapes are invariant on size transformations. Shapes can be subdivided into 2D and 3D shapes, We can also make a distinction between shapes of complete self-connected objects, and shapes of parts of objects.

annotations.label: trophic quality
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to synthesize a particular organic compound required for its growth.

annotations.label: occurrence
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's occurrence.

annotations.label: odor
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's molecules being are aerially dispersed and perceived by an odorant receptor.

annotations.label: spatial pattern
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting repetition of placement of its parts.

annotations.label: sleep pattern
annotations.definition: A sleep quality defined by the mathematic properties of the relative time frames of the sleep cycle.

annotations.label: pilosity
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having hair or bristles.

annotations.label: qualitative
annotations.definition: A quality of an entity that is descriptive or observational, typically not represented numerically.
annotations.comment: TODO: define this or obsolete it and move children somewhere else.

annotations.label: deviation (from_normal)
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the whether the bearer differs from normal or average.

annotations.label: amount
annotations.definition: The number of entities of this type that are part of the whole organism.
annotations.comment: This term was originally named "presence". It has been renamed to reduce ambiguity. Consider annotating with the reciprocal relation,PATO:0001555, has_number_of. For example, rather than E=fin ray Q=count in organism C=10, say E=organism Q=has number of E2= fin ray C=10.

annotations.label: response to
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the whether the bearer's disposition to react to a stimulus or an agent.

annotations.label: rhythm quality
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's movement or variation characterized by the regular recurrence or alternation of different quantities or conditions.

annotations.label: amplitude
annotations.definition: A physical quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the size of the bearer's maximum displacement from the 'normal' position, when periodic motion is taking place.

annotations.label: persistence
annotations.definition: A rhythm quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the repetitiveness of bearer's rhythm.

annotations.label: phase
annotations.definition: A quality that exists by virtue of being a particular point in the time of a cycle.

annotations.label: sensitivity toward
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to detect or perceive external stimulation.

annotations.label: size
annotations.definition: A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's physical magnitude.

annotations.label: height
annotations.definition: A 1-D extent quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's vertical dimension of extension.

annotations.label: length
annotations.definition: A 1-D extent quality which is equal to the distance between two points.
annotations.comment: Length often refers to the longer or longest dimension of an object, however, this is not always true. See https://github.com/pato-ontology/pato/issues/337 for full discussion.

annotations.label: mass
annotations.definition: A physical quality that inheres in a bearer by virtue of the proportion of the bearer's amount of matter.
annotations.comment: For any biological use of PATO, the concept that should be used is 'mass' and not 'weight'. For example, increased weight of an organism.

annotations.label: weight
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer that has mass near a gravitational body. Important usage note: Note that this ontology has a class for a similar concept, mass (PATO:0000125). Mass and weight are not equivalent. For most use cases, the class mass is strongly preferred over weight.
annotations.comment: For any biological use of PATO, the concept that should be used is 'mass' and not 'weight'. For example, increased weight of an organism.

annotations.label: orientation
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's placement which is defined by the angle between the bearer and an axis, or the angle between the bearer and another object.

annotations.label: closure
annotations.definition: A morphological quality pertaining to the degree to which an object contains an opening, aperture, orifice or vent.

annotations.label: position
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's spatial location relative to other objects in the vicinity.

annotations.label: structure
annotations.definition: A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's relative position, shape, arrangements and connectivity of an organism's various parts; the pattern underlying its form.

annotations.label: temperature
annotations.definition: A physical quality of the thermal energy of a system.

annotations.label: texture
annotations.definition: A morphologic quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's relative size, organization and distribution of its surface elements or the representation or invention of the appearance of its surface; visual and tactile surface characteristics.

annotations.label: threshold
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's sensitivity towards a fixed location or value where a change is observed; upper limit.

annotations.label: rate
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's occurrence per unit time.

annotations.label: time
annotations.definition: A quality in which events occur in sequence.

annotations.label: viability
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer or a population by virtue of the bearer's disposition to survive and develop normally or the number of surviving individuals in a given population.

annotations.label: balance
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of having physical steadiness.

annotations.label: behavioral quality
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's behavior aggregate of the responses or reactions or movements in a given situation.

annotations.label: coordination
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or lacking skillful and effective interaction of movement.

annotations.label: discrimination
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer's disposition to perceive differences between two or more stimuli.

annotations.label: maturity
annotations.definition: A quality of a single physical entity which is held by a bearer when the latter exhibits a state of growth, differentiation, or development.

annotations.label: fecundity
annotations.definition: A reproductive quality inhering in an organism or population by virtue of the bearer's potential reproductive capacity ad measured by the number of gametes.

annotations.label: fertility
annotations.definition: A reproductive quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's initiating, sustaining, or supporting reproduction.

annotations.label: brood size
annotations.definition: A reproductive quality inhering in the young of an animal by virtue of their number.

annotations.label: female fertility
annotations.definition: A fertility quality inhering in a female by virtue of the bearer's disposition to initiate, sustain, or support reproduction.

annotations.label: male fertility
annotations.definition: A fertility quality of inhering in a male by virtue of the bearer's disposition to initiate, sustain, or support reproduction.

annotations.label: arrested
annotations.definition: A quality of a process which ends earlier than the natural end time or reference process.
annotations.comment: TODO: the definition is incorrect. See absent.

annotations.label: mobile
annotations.definition: A mobility quality of inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to move freely.

annotations.label: immobile
annotations.definition: A mobility quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being incapable to move freely.

annotations.label: increased speed
annotations.definition: A speed which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased speed
annotations.definition: A speed which is relatively low.

annotations.label: old
annotations.definition: An age which is relatively high.

annotations.label: young
annotations.definition: An age which is relatively low.

annotations.label: black
annotations.definition: A color that lacks any hues as parts.

annotations.label: blue
annotations.definition: A color hue with low wavelength of that portion of the visible spectrum lying between green and indigo, evoked in the human observer by radiant energy with wavelengths of approximately 420 to 490 nanometers.

annotations.label: cyan
annotations.definition: A color consisting of green and blue hue.

annotations.label: green
annotations.definition: A color hue with medium-low wavelength of that portion of the visible spectrum lying between yellow and blue, evoked in the human observer by radiant energy with wavelengths of approximately 490 to 570 nanometers.

annotations.label: magenta
annotations.definition: A color consisting of red and blue hues.

annotations.label: red
annotations.definition: A color hue with high wavelength of the long-wave end of the visible spectrum, evoked in the human observer by radiant energy with wavelengths of approximately 630 to 750 nanometers.

annotations.label: white
annotations.definition: An achromatic color of maximum brightness; the color of objects that reflect nearly all light of all visible wavelengths.

annotations.label: yellow
annotations.definition: A color hue with medium wavelength of that portion of the visible spectrum lying between orange and green, evoked in the human observer by radiant energy with wavelengths of approximately 570 to 590 nanometers.

annotations.label: low brightness
annotations.definition: A color brightness which is relatively low.

annotations.label: low saturation
annotations.definition: A color saturation which is of low purity.

annotations.label: blotchy
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's being marked with irregularly shaped spots or blots of a different hue or degree of saturation.

annotations.label: irregular spatial pattern
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's magnitude of or the relationships between its repeated parts lack consistency.

annotations.label: discolored
annotations.definition: A color quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being altered or spoiled in color.

annotations.label: spotted
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's being marked with a round area of different hue or degree of saturation.

annotations.label: colored
annotations.definition: A color quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having color.

annotations.label: colorless
annotations.definition: A color quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking color.

annotations.label: compatible
annotations.definition: A compatibility quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of harmonious coexistence.

annotations.label: incompatible
annotations.definition: A compatibility quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being incapable of harmonious coexistence.

annotations.label: down
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's direction from a higher to a lower point.

annotations.label: left
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's direction that is similar to the direction of an object to the north when it faces east.

annotations.label: right
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's direction that is similar to the direction of an object to the north when it faces west.

annotations.label: up
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's direction from a lower to a higher point.

annotations.label: increased distance
annotations.definition: A distance which is greater relative to the normal or average.

annotations.label: decreased distance
annotations.definition: A distance which is lesser relative to the normal or average.

annotations.label: increased frequency
annotations.definition: A frequency which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased frequency
annotations.definition: A frequency which is relatively low.

annotations.label: female
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population that only produces gametes that can be fertilised by male gametes.

annotations.label: male
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population whose sex organs contain only male gametes.

annotations.label: hard
annotations.definition: A hardness quality of being rigid and resistant to pressure.

annotations.label: soft
annotations.definition: A hardness quality of giving little resistance to pressure.

annotations.label: acute
annotations.definition: A quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a sudden onset, sharp rise, and short course.

annotations.label: mild intensity
annotations.definition: An intensity which is less than moderate in type or degree or effect or force.

annotations.label: moderate intensity
annotations.definition: An intensity which is less than extreme in type or degree or effect or force.

annotations.label: severe intensity
annotations.definition: An intensity which is extremely bad or unpleasant in type or degree or effect or force.

annotations.label: cleft
annotations.definition: A concave quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a groove or fissure.

annotations.label: coiled
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curled or wound (especially in concentric rings or spirals).

annotations.label: curled
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having parallel chains in undulate fashion on the border.

annotations.label: curved
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or being marked by a curve or smoothly rounded bend.

annotations.label: flat
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a horizontal surface without a slope, tilt, or curvature.

annotations.label: oblate
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting an equatorial diameter greater than its polar diameter.

annotations.label: pinnate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having leaflets or leaflets-like structures on each side of a common axis.

annotations.label: circular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being such that every part of the surface or the circumference is equidistant from the center.

annotations.label: square
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an equal-sided rectangular form.

annotations.label: unbranched
annotations.definition: A branchiness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking branches.

annotations.label: uncoiled
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being not curled or wound (especially in concentric rings or spirals).

annotations.label: auxotrophic
annotations.definition: A nutritional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's inability to synthesize a particular organic compound required for its growth.

annotations.label: prototrophic
annotations.definition: A nutritional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to synthesize a particular organic compound required for its growth.

annotations.label: recurrent
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's occurring or appearing again or repeatedly.

annotations.label: sporadic
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's once or in or a few unpredictable instances.

annotations.label: invariant
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking or not exhibiting variation.

annotations.label: regular spatial pattern
annotations.definition: A pattern quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a repeatable or predictable placement.

annotations.label: repetitive
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's marked by tedious repetition.

annotations.label: glabrous
annotations.definition: A pilosity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lack of hairs or bristles.

annotations.label: hairy
annotations.definition: A pilosity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered with hair or hairlike projections.

annotations.label: pubescent
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having arrived at the onset of puberty (the age at which sex glands become functional) but not yet fully mature.

annotations.label: abnormal
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's deviation from normal or average.

annotations.label: normal
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting no deviation from normal or average.

annotations.label: absent
annotations.definition: A quality denoting the lack of an entity.
annotations.comment: We recommend using the new absent terms (physical absence, absent anatomical entity, or absent process) instead of this class. This is a legacy term and will ultimately be obsoleted.\nSee https://github.com/pato-ontology/pato/issues/331.

annotations.label: conspicuous
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of being clearly visible.

annotations.label: inconspicuous
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of not being clearly visible.

annotations.label: present
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's existence.

annotations.label: increased amount
annotations.definition: An amount which is relatively high.

synonyms.text: present in greater numbers in organism

annotations.label: responsive to
annotations.definition: A response quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to react to a stimulus or an agent.

annotations.label: unresponsive to
annotations.definition: A response quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lack of reaction to a stimulus or an agent.

annotations.label: increased duration
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased duration
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process which is relatively low.

annotations.label: advanced phase
annotations.definition: A phase which occurs earlier than the natural start time.

annotations.label: dark phase
annotations.definition: A phase which occurs during dark cycle.

annotations.label: delayed
annotations.definition: A quality of a process which starts later than the natural start time or the reference process.

annotations.label: light phase
annotations.definition: A phase which occurs during the light cycle.

annotations.label: arrhythmic
annotations.definition: A rhythm quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking rhythm.

annotations.label: rhythmic
annotations.definition: A rhythm quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having rhythm.

annotations.label: insensitive toward
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking sensitivity toward an external stimulus.

annotations.label: tolerant to
annotations.definition: A resistance quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its disposition to endure or being insensitive to a stimulus.

annotations.label: sensitive toward
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having sensitivity toward an external stimulus.

annotations.label: photoinsensitive
annotations.definition: A photosensitivity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking photosensitivity.

annotations.label: photosensitive
annotations.definition: A photosensitivity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting photosensitivity.

annotations.label: decreased height
annotations.definition: A height which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased height
annotations.definition: A height which is relatively high.

annotations.label: increased length
annotations.definition: A length quality which is relatively large.

annotations.label: decreased length
annotations.definition: A length quality which is relatively small.

annotations.label: increased weight
annotations.definition: A weight which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased weight
annotations.definition: A weight which is relatively low.

annotations.label: hypertrophic
annotations.definition: An increased size quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting enlargement of a cell or constituent group of cells (for example, organ).

annotations.label: hypotrophic
annotations.definition: A decreased size quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting reduced size of a cell or constituent group of cells (for example, organ).

annotations.label: increased size
annotations.definition: A size quality which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased size
annotations.definition: A size quality which is relatively low.

annotations.label: vestigial
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being a remnant structure from earlier development or evolution.
annotations.comment: May be part of an animal (such as an organ or bone) that is no longer used by the species and has therefore become smaller or less developed.

annotations.label: increased thickness
annotations.definition: A thickness which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased thickness
annotations.definition: A thickness which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased volume
annotations.definition: A volume which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased volume
annotations.definition: A volume which is relatively low.

annotations.label: decreased width
annotations.definition: A width which is relatively small.

annotations.label: increased width
annotations.definition: A width which is relatively large.

annotations.label: closed
annotations.definition: A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's affording blocked passage or view.

annotations.label: closure incomplete
annotations.definition: A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's affording not completed blocked passage or view.

annotations.label: open
annotations.definition: A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's affording unobstructed passage or view.

annotations.label: disoriented
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking orientation.

annotations.label: oriented
annotations.definition: A pattern where all the repeated elements are oriented in the same direction.

annotations.label: wholly anteriorized
annotations.definition: An anteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing only what are normally anterior structures.

annotations.label: asymmetrical
annotations.definition: A symmetry quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking symmetry.

annotations.label: bent
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having one or more angle(s) in its length.

annotations.label: bilateral
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two sides; two-sided.

annotations.label: crowded
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being overfilled.

annotations.label: wholly dorsalized
annotations.definition: A dorsalized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing only what are normally dorsal structures.

annotations.label: erect
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being upright in position or posture.

annotations.label: exserted
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's protruding or projecting from the body.

annotations.label: inserted into
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's becoming joined together with an additional entity.

annotations.label: inverted
annotations.definition: An oriented quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being reversed in position, order, or condition.

annotations.label: lateralized
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being moved or displaced to one side so as to make lateral.

annotations.label: localized
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being confined or restricted to a particular location.

annotations.label: mislocalised
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue the bearer's being changed in abnormal position.

annotations.label: misrouted
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having mistakenly taken course, way, or passage; ill-routed.

annotations.label: wholly posteriorized
annotations.definition: A posteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing only what are normally posterior structures.

annotations.label: prostrate
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being stretched out and lying at full length along the ground.

annotations.label: symmetrical
annotations.definition: A symmetry quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of division by a longitudinal plane into similar halves.
annotations.comment: similar pattern in both halves

annotations.label: uncrowded
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being not overfilled.

annotations.label: unilateral
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's involvement of only one part or side.

annotations.label: unlocalised
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not being confined or restricted to a particular location.

annotations.label: wholly ventralized
annotations.definition: A ventralized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing only what are normally ventral structures.

annotations.label: apoptotic
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting of blebbing of the nucleus and DNA fragmentation due to the cell undergoing a specific form of programmed cell death termed apoptosis.

annotations.label: degenerate
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer whose structure deteriorates or is lost over time due to an active pathological process.

annotations.label: dysplastic
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting an alteration in size, shape or organization of its constituent cells.

annotations.label: fused with
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being merged with another entity.

annotations.label: herniated
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's bodily structure that has protruded through an abnormal opening in the wall that contains it.

annotations.label: hyperplastic
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in an organ or tissue by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting an increased number of cells, typically associated with an increase in size.

annotations.label: hypoplastic
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in an organ or tissue by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a decreased number of cells, typically associated with a decrease in size.

annotations.label: malformed
annotations.definition: A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being distorted during formation.

annotations.label: necrotic
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's undergoing unprogrammed cell death.

annotations.label: obstructed
annotations.definition: A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being blocked or filled with obstacles or an obstacle.

annotations.label: unfused from
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being no longer merged with another entity.

annotations.label: high brightness
annotations.definition: A color brightness which is relatively high.

annotations.label: asynchronous
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not occurring or existing at the same time or having the same period or phase.

annotations.label: continuous
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being uninterrupted in time, sequence, substance, or extent.

annotations.label: discontinuous
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by breaks or interruptions.

annotations.label: heterochronic
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's origin or development at an unusual time or out of the regular sequence.

annotations.label: premature
annotations.definition: A quality of a process which starts earlier than the natural start time or the reference process.

annotations.label: synchronous
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's occurring or existing at the same time or having the same period or phase.

annotations.label: rough
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's irregular surface.

annotations.label: smooth
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's processing a surface free of roughness or irregularities.

annotations.label: increased threshold
annotations.definition: A threshold which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased threshold
annotations.definition: A threshold which is relatively low.

annotations.label: lethal (sensu genetics)
annotations.definition: A viability quality inhering in a population by virtue of the bearer's long term survival inability.

annotations.label: viable
annotations.definition: A viability quality inhering in a bearer or a population by virtue of the bearer's ability to survive or the long term survival ability of a given population.

annotations.label: balanced
annotations.definition: A balance quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having balance.

annotations.label: unbalanced
annotations.definition: A balance quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking balance.

annotations.label: bang sensitive
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting paralytic behaviour when subjected to mechanical shock.

annotations.label: increased behavioural activity
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting increased activity.

annotations.label: decreased behavioural activity
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting reduced activity.

annotations.label: paralysed
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being affected by loss of the ability to move a body part.

annotations.label: passive
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's receiving or being subjected to an action without responding or initiating an action in return.

annotations.label: coordinated
annotations.definition: A coordination quality of inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having skillful and effective interaction of movement.

annotations.label: uncoordinated
annotations.definition: A coordination quality of inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking skillful and effective interaction of movement.

annotations.label: aversion
annotations.definition: A preference quality in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disliking a perceived stimulus.

annotations.label: indifference
annotations.definition: A preference quality in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having no preference to a perceived stimulus.

annotations.label: preference
annotations.definition: A discrimination quality in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's liking a perceived stimulus.

annotations.label: female fertile
annotations.definition: A female fertility quality inhering in a female by virtue of the bearer's disposition to initiate, sustain, or support reproduction.

annotations.label: male sterile
annotations.definition: A male fertility quality inhering in a male by virtue of the bearer's being incapable of initiating, sustaining, or supporting reproduction.

annotations.label: male fertile
annotations.definition: A male fertility quality inhering in a male by virtue of the bearer's being capable of initiating, sustaining, or supporting reproduction.

annotations.label: female sterile
annotations.definition: A female fertility quality inhering in a female by virtue of the bearer's being incapable of initiating, sustaining, or supporting reproduction.

annotations.label: decreased rate
annotations.definition: A rate which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased rate
annotations.definition: A rate which is relatively high.

annotations.label: thickness
annotations.definition: A 1-D extent quality which is equal to the dimension through an object as opposed to its length or width.
annotations.comment: Thickness often refers to the smallest 1-D extent compared to the other two orthogonal dimensions, however, this is not always true. See https://github.com/pato-ontology/pato/issues/337 for full discussion.

annotations.label: volume
annotations.definition: A 3-D extent quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's amount of 3-dimensional space it occupies.

annotations.label: width
annotations.definition: A 1-D extent quality which is equal to the distance from one side of an object to another side which is opposite.
annotations.comment: Width often refers to the dimension that is greater than the extent in only one of the other two orthogonal dimensions, however, this is not always true. See https://github.com/pato-ontology/pato/issues/337 for full discussion.

annotations.label: photosensitivity
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to being sensitivity to the action of radiant energy.

annotations.label: truncated
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's terminating abruptly by having or as if having an end or point cut off.

annotations.label: disorganized
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking organisation.

annotations.label: organized
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting organisation.

annotations.label: vacuolated
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a cytoplasm that contains fluid filled cavities.

annotations.label: sharpness
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a sharp or tapered end or point.

annotations.label: epinastic
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a downward bending of its leaves or other plant parts.

annotations.label: oblong
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a somewhat elongated form with approximately parallel sides.

annotations.label: elliptic
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being oval with two axes of symmetry, as produced by a conical section.

annotations.label: heart shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a sinus or rounded lobe at the base.
annotations.comment: Having the shape of heart.

annotations.label: fasciated
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being abnormally flattened or coalesced.

annotations.label: grey
annotations.definition: A color between white and black colors.

annotations.label: purple
annotations.definition: A color that falls about midway between red and blue in hue.

annotations.label: brown
annotations.definition: A color consisting of dark orange, red, of very low intensity.

annotations.label: orange
annotations.definition: A color hue with high-medium wavelength that of that portion of the visible spectrum lying between red and yellow, evoked in the human observer by radiant energy with wavelengths of approximately 585 to 620 nanometers.

annotations.label: pink
annotations.definition: Red color having medium to high brightness and low to moderate saturation.

annotations.label: fertile
annotations.definition: A fertility quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of initiating, sustaining, or supporting reproduction.

annotations.label: sterile
annotations.definition: A fertility quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being incapable of initiating, sustaining, or supporting reproduction.

annotations.label: opacity
annotations.definition: An optical quality which obtains by virtue of the ability of the bearer to absorb visible light.

annotations.label: opaque
annotations.definition: A optical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not being clear; not transmitting or reflecting light or radiant energy.

annotations.label: transparent
annotations.definition: A optical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking opacity.

annotations.label: symmetry
annotations.definition: A pattern quality of inhering in a bearer by virtue of the correspondence in size, shape, and relative position of the bearer's parts on opposite sides of a dividing line or median plane or about a center or axis.

annotations.label: undulate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a sinuate margin and rippled surface.

annotations.label: dwarf-like
annotations.definition: A size quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being abnormally small.

annotations.label: permeability
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to being permeated or pervaded by a gas or liquid (as by osmosis or diffusion).

annotations.label: porosity
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to admit the passage of gas or liquid through pores or interstices.

annotations.label: permeable
annotations.definition: A permeability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable to be permeated or pervaded by a gas or liquid (as by osmosis or diffusion).

annotations.label: impermeable
annotations.definition: A permeability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being incapable of being permeated or pervaded by a gas or liquid (as by osmosis or diffusion).

annotations.label: porous
annotations.definition: A porosity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of admitting the passage of gas or liquid through pores or interstices.

annotations.label: non-porous
annotations.definition: A porosity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being incapable of admitting the passage of gas or liquid through pores or interstices.

annotations.label: viscosity
annotations.definition: A physical quality of a fluid inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to internal resistance to flow.

annotations.label: viscous
annotations.definition: A viscosity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having viscosity.

annotations.label: alternation
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's successive change from one thing or state to another and back again.

annotations.label: latency
annotations.definition: A time quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the time it elapses for the bearer to respond to a stimulus.
annotations.comment: This class is a candidate for obsoletion. The definition is not clear, and the placement under "delayed" is not consistent with the definition.

annotations.label: physical quality
annotations.definition: A quality of a physical entity that exists through action of continuants at the physical level of organisation in relation to other entities.

annotations.label: mass density
annotations.definition: A physical quality which inheres in a bearer by virtue of some influence is exerted by the bearer's mass per unit size.

annotations.label: damage
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer has been harmed or injured or spoiled.

annotations.label: energy
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's capacity to do work.

annotations.label: impulse
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the amount of momentum caused a certain force will produce over time.

annotations.label: momentum
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's velocity multiplied by its mass.

annotations.label: power
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's rate of doing work.

annotations.label: pressure
annotations.definition: A physical quality that inheres in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's amount of force per unit area it exerts.

annotations.label: work
annotations.definition: A physical quality which is equal to the energy transferred by a force to a moving object.

annotations.label: acceleration
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the rate of change of the bearer's velocity in either speed or direction.

annotations.label: efficiency
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of ratio of the bearer's output to the bearer's input.

annotations.label: flux
annotations.definition: A physical quality that exists by virtue of the rate of flow of the bearer across a given surface.

annotations.label: elasticity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to recover its size and shape after deformation in any way.

annotations.label: strain
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the relative change in the bearer's length or the bearer's volume when being stretched or squashed.

annotations.label: force
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's rate of change of momentum.

annotations.label: susceptibility toward
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in an entity by virtue of the bearer's propensity to resist an external stimulus.

annotations.label: resistance to
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to resist to a stimulus.

annotations.label: acute angle to
annotations.definition: An angle which is less than 90 degrees.

annotations.label: obtuse angle to
annotations.definition: An angle which is between 90 degrees and 180 degrees to another entity.

annotations.label: convex angle to
annotations.definition: An angle which is less than 180 degrees to another entity.

annotations.label: internal angle
annotations.definition: A convex angle that is inside two adjacent sides of a polygon.

annotations.label: reflex angle to
annotations.definition: An angular placement quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's placement at an angle that is greater than 180 degrees but less than 360 degrees to another entity.

annotations.label: susceptible toward
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having susceptibilty toward an external stimulus.

annotations.label: insusceptible toward
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking susceptibilty toward an external stimulus.

annotations.label: elongated
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's length being notably higher than its width.

annotations.label: concentrated
annotations.definition: A concentration quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting concentration.

annotations.label: diluted
annotations.definition: A concentration which relatively low.

annotations.label: increased concentration
annotations.definition: A concentration which is higher relative to the normal or average.

annotations.label: decreased concentration
annotations.definition: A concentration which is lower relative to the normal or average.

annotations.label: dense
annotations.definition: A physical quality which inheres in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting density.

annotations.label: damaged
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being harmed or injured or spoiled, such that its functionality is impaired.

annotations.label: undamaged
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of not being harmed or injured or spoiled.

annotations.label: elastic
annotations.definition: An elasticity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to recover its size and shape after deformation in any way.

annotations.label: inelastic
annotations.definition: An elasticity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's inability to recover its size and shape after deformation in any way.

annotations.label: resistant to
annotations.definition: A resistance quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resistance to a stimulus.

annotations.label: larval
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's indirect development, undergoing metamorphosis.

annotations.label: prepubescent
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being at the age immediately before puberty.

annotations.label: pupal
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering in a insect by virtue of the bearer's being in the chrysalis (cocoon) or post larval stage.

annotations.label: prepupal
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being in an inactive stage in the development of some insects, between the larval and the pupal stages.

annotations.label: adolescent
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being between the onset of puberty and maturity.

annotations.label: juvenile
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue the bearer's being not fully grown or developed.

annotations.label: medial to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the middle relative to another entity.

annotations.label: hyperresponsive to
annotations.definition: A response quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's excessive reaction to a stimulus or an agent.

annotations.label: lateral to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the side relative to another entity.

annotations.label: hyporesponsive to
annotations.definition: A response quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's limited reaction to a stimulus or an agent.

annotations.label: proximal to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located more centrally than another entity.

annotations.label: ventral to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the abdomen of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: T-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of the letter T.

annotations.label: Y-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of the letter Y.

annotations.label: dentated
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having toothlike projections in the margin.

annotations.label: serrated
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of having sharp straight-edged teeth pointing to the apex.

annotations.label: odorless
annotations.definition: An odor quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking odour.

annotations.label: variant
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits variation or change.

annotations.label: high saturation
annotations.definition: A color saturation which is of high purity.

annotations.label: strength
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's power or force.

annotations.label: dorsal to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the back or upper surface of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: distal to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located further from a more centrally located entity.

annotations.label: process quality
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in an process.
annotations.comment: See comments of relational quality of a physical entity.

annotations.label: physical object quality
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in a continuant.
annotations.comment: Relational qualities are qualities that hold between multiple entities. Normal (monadic) qualities such as the shape of a eyeball exist purely as a quality of that eyeball. A relational quality such as sensitivity to light is a quality of that eyeball (and connecting nervous system) as it relates to incoming light waves/particles.

synonyms.text: multiply inhering quality of a physical entity
quality of a single physical entity

annotations.label: wavelength
annotations.definition: A physical quality which is equal to the distance between repeating units of a wave pattern.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of magenta with low brightness.

annotations.definition: Pink color having medium brightness and moderate saturation.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of red hue and high brightness.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of yellow hue and high brightness.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of blue hue and high saturation.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of cyan colour and high saturation.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of green hue and high saturation.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of orange hue and low saturation.

annotations.definition: Pink color having medium brightness and low saturation.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of purple color and high saturation.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of red hue and high saturation.

annotations.definition: A color consisting of yellow hue and high saturation.
