annotations.definition: A color consisting of red and brown hue.

annotations.label: dark red brown
annotations.definition: A color consisting of red and brown hue and low brightness.

annotations.label: light red brown
annotations.definition: A color consisting of red and brown hue and high brightness.

annotations.label: fluorescent
annotations.definition: A fluorescence quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of emitting light during exposure to radiation from an external source.

annotations.label: full-spectrum EM radiation quality
annotations.definition: An EM radiation quality that is independent of the EM wavelength range.

annotations.label: absorption quality
annotations.definition: A scalar EM radiation quality which obtains by the capacity of the bearer to retain radiation.

annotations.label: radiation reflective quality
annotations.definition: A scalar EM radiation quality which obtains by the capacity of the bearer to scatter or reflect radiation.

annotations.label: albedo
annotations.definition: A reflective quality restricted to a particular wavelength.
annotations.comment: Typically used for light, but also applied to full EM spectrum.

annotations.label: luminous flux
annotations.definition: A scalar optical quality which obtains by the magnitude of the light emitted by the bearer.

annotations.label: reflectivity
annotations.definition: A radiation reflective quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the ratio of the energy of a wave reflected from its surface to the energy possessed by the wave striking the bearer's surface.

annotations.label: phosphorescence
annotations.definition: A fluorescence in which the emittence continues after the absorption has ceased.

annotations.label: radiation emitting quality
annotations.definition: A scalar EM radiation quality which obtains by the capacity of the bearer to emit radiation.

annotations.label: optical quality
annotations.definition: An EM radiation quality in which the EM radiation is within the fiat range of the spectrum visible deemed to be light.

annotations.label: chromatic property
annotations.definition: An optical quality that is the mixture, purity or pattern of wavelengths of light perceived by the observer.

annotations.label: vermilion
annotations.definition: A color consisting of red and orange hue with a slight amount of gray.

annotations.label: variability
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to varying or changing.

annotations.label: variability of temperature
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits temperature variation or change.

annotations.label: increased temperature
annotations.definition: A temperature which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased temperature
annotations.definition: A temperature which is relatively low.

annotations.label: decreased variability of temperature
annotations.definition: A variability of temperature which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased variability of temperature
annotations.definition: A variability of temperature which is relatively high.

annotations.label: duration
annotations.definition: A process quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's magnitude of the temporal extent between the starting and ending point.

annotations.label: duration of temperature
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's duration of exhibition of thermal energy.
annotations.comment: TODO: obsolete? I don't know what this means.

annotations.label: decreased duration of temperature
annotations.definition: A duration of temperature which is lesser relative to the normal or average enduring or continuing in time.

annotations.label: increased duration of temperature
annotations.definition: A duration of temperature which is greater relative to the normal or average in respect to the quality of temperature of enduring or continuing in time.

annotations.label: invariant temperature
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking or not exhibiting variation of temperature.

annotations.label: variant temperature
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or exhibiting variation of temperature.

annotations.label: mild increased temperature
annotations.definition: A temperature which is increased by a low degree.

annotations.label: moderate increased temperature
annotations.definition: A temperature which is increased by a medium degree.

annotations.label: severe increased temperature
annotations.definition: A temperature which is increased by a high degree.

annotations.label: indiscriminate
annotations.definition: A discrimination quality in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being incapable perceiving differences between two or more stimuli.

annotations.label: discriminate
annotations.definition: A discrimination quality in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of perceiving differences between two or more stimuli.

annotations.label: pubescent hair
annotations.definition: A pilosity quality of being covered with short hairs or soft down.

annotations.label: right angle to
annotations.definition: An angle which is 90 degrees to another entity.

annotations.label: straight angle to
annotations.definition: An angle which is 180 degrees to another entity.
annotations.comment: TODO: decide on correct parentage.

annotations.label: area
annotations.definition: A 2-D extent quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's two dimensional extent.

annotations.label: bilateral symmetry
annotations.definition: A symmetry quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being symmetric about a plane running from its frontal end to its caudal end (head to tail), and has nearly identical right and left halves.

annotations.label: radial symmetry
annotations.definition: A symmetry quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having equal proportion around a central point or axis.

annotations.label: zygomorphic
annotations.definition: A symmetry quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of division into symmetrical halves by only one longitudinal plane passing through the axis.

annotations.label: actinomorphic
annotations.definition: A symmetry quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of division into symmetrical halves by any longitudinal plane passing through the axis.

annotations.label: flavourful
annotations.definition: A flavor quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having flavour.

annotations.label: flavourless
annotations.definition: A flavor quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking flavour.

annotations.label: odorous
annotations.definition: An odor quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having odour.

annotations.label: amorphous
annotations.definition: A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lack of distinct morphology.

annotations.label: temporally extended
annotations.definition: A quality of a process which ends later than the natural end time.

annotations.label: diameter
annotations.definition: A length quality which is equal to the length of any straight line segment that passes through the center of a circle and whose endpoints are on the circular boundary.

annotations.label: bacterial mating type
annotations.definition: A mating type that indicates whether the F plasmid has integrated into the chromosome.

annotations.label: yeast mating type
annotations.definition: A yeast mating type.

annotations.label: mixed sex
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in a population of multiple sexes.
annotations.comment: For example a mixture of females and male or males and hermaphrodites.

annotations.label: biomaterial purity
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's homogeneity of a biomaterial.

annotations.label: hermaphrodite
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an organism or a population with both male and female sexual organs in one individual.

annotations.label: a mating type (yeast)
annotations.definition: A S. cerevisiae mating type cells that secrete a pheromone that in alpha haploids stimulates processes that lead to mating.

annotations.label: Saccharomyces cerevisiae mating type
annotations.definition: A S. cerevisiae mating type.

annotations.label: Schizosaccharomyces pombe mating type
annotations.definition: A S. pombe mating type determined by the gene configuration on the mat1 locus.

annotations.label: alpha mating type (yeast)
annotations.definition: A S. cerevisiae mating type cells that secrete a pheromone that stimulates a haploids.

annotations.label: h minus
annotations.definition: A S. pombe mating type determined by the mat1-Mc and mat1-Mi on the mat1 locus.

annotations.label: h plus
annotations.definition: A S. pombe mating type determined by the mat1-Pc and mat1-Pi on the mat1 locus.

annotations.label: F mating type
annotations.definition: A bacterial mating type indicating the presence of F plasmid in a bacterial cell.

annotations.label: F minus mating type
annotations.definition: A bacterial mating type indicating the absence of F plasmid in a bacterial cell.

annotations.label: high frequency recombinant
annotations.definition: A mating type that indicates that the F plasmid has integrated into the chromosome.

annotations.label: angular acceleration
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the rate of change of the bearer's angular velocity.

annotations.label: area density
annotations.definition: A density quality which inheres in a bearer by virtue of some influence exerted by the bearer's mass on a given area.

annotations.label: linear density
annotations.definition: A density quality which is equal to the mass exerting an influence on a one-dimensional object.

annotations.label: volumetric density
annotations.definition: A density quality which inheres in a bearer by virtue of some influence exerted by the bearer's mass on a given volume.

annotations.label: translucent
annotations.definition: A optical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting low opacity.

annotations.label: convex
annotations.definition: A shape quality that obtains by virtue of the bearer having inward facing edges; having a surface or boundary that curves or bulges outward, as the exterior of a sphere.

annotations.label: pleomorphic
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a cell by virtue of the bearer's ability to take on two or more different shapes during its life cycle.

annotations.label: pulvinate
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped like a cushion or has a marked convex cushion-like form.

annotations.label: umbonate
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a knob or knoblike protuberance.

annotations.label: rugose
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having many wrinkles or creases on the surface.

annotations.label: filamentous
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having thin filamentous extensions at its edge.

annotations.label: warty
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered with warts or projections that resemble warts resulting in a hard rough surface.

annotations.label: fragile
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to being easily damaged or destroyed.

annotations.label: rhizoidal
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having root like extensions radiating from its center.

annotations.label: spiny
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having spines, thorns or similar thin projections on its surface.

annotations.label: punctiform
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being small and resembling a point.

annotations.label: lobate
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having deeply undulating edges forming lobes.

annotations.label: erose
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an irregularly toothed edge.

annotations.label: raised
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a colony by virtue of the bearer's appearing above the medium surface with terraced edges.

annotations.label: viscid
annotations.definition: A coating quality which is sticky or clammy.

annotations.label: mucoid
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's consistency of mucus.

annotations.label: refractivity
annotations.definition: A physical quality that inheres in propagating wave (light or sound) virtue of the bearer's change in direction when passing from one medium to another.

annotations.label: glistening
annotations.definition: A reflectivity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's reflecting lots of light.

annotations.label: ploidy
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's number of homologous sets of chromosomes in the nucleus or primary chromosome-containing compartment of the cell, each set essentially coding for all the biological traits of the organism.

annotations.label: haploid
annotations.definition: A ploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing a single set of homologous chromosomes.

annotations.label: monoploid
annotations.definition: A ploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing a single set of unique homologous chromosomes.

annotations.label: polyploid
annotations.definition: A ploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing more than two homologous sets of chromosomes.

annotations.label: autopolyploid
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing chromosomes derived from a single species.

annotations.label: allopolyploidy
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing chromosomes derived from different species.

annotations.label: paleopolyploid
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an ancient polyploid ancestor.

annotations.label: triploid
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing three homologous sets of chromosomes.

annotations.label: tetraploid
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing four homologous sets of chromosomes.

annotations.label: pentaploid
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing five homologous sets of chromosomes.

annotations.label: hexaploid
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing six homologous sets of chromosomes.

annotations.label: aneuploid
annotations.definition: A ploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing a non-integral multiple of the monoploid number, due to extra or missing chromosomes.

annotations.label: monosomy
annotations.definition: An aneuploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing only one chromosome from a pair in a cell's nucleus.

annotations.label: disomy
annotations.definition: An aneuploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing only two chromosome from a pair in a cell's nucleus.
annotations.comment: For diploid organisms, such as humans, it is the normal condition, whilst for organisms that are normally triploid or above, disomy is an aneuploidy.

annotations.label: uniparental disomy
annotations.definition: A disomy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing two copies of the chromosome from one of the parents (with no contribution from the other parent).

annotations.label: trisomy
annotations.definition: An aneuploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing three, instead of two, chromosomes of a particular numbered type in an organism.

annotations.label: partial trisomy
annotations.definition: A trisomy quality inhering in a bearer when part of the bearer's extra chromosome is attached to one of the other chromosomes, or if one of the bearer's chromosomes has two copies of part of its chromosome.

annotations.label: mosaic trisomy
annotations.definition: A trisomy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having extra chromosomal material in only some of it's cells.

annotations.label: endopolyploid
annotations.definition: A polyploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's chromosome number being increased by endomitosis and for which the degree of ploidy is proportional to the number of times that endomitosis has taken place.

annotations.label: euploid
annotations.definition: A ploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing an integral multiple of the monoploid number, possibly excluding the sex-determining chromosomes.

annotations.label: diploid
annotations.definition: A ploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two copies (homologs) of each chromosome, usually one from the mother and one from the father.
annotations.comment: The exact number may be one or two different from the 2n number and still be classified as diploidy (although with aneuploidy). Nearly all mammals are diploid organisms, although all individuals have some small fraction of cells that are polyploidy.

annotations.label: haplodiploid
annotations.definition: A diploidy quality inhering in a bearer in by virtue of belonging in a species whose one of the sexes has haploid cells and the other has diploid cells.

annotations.label: cellular quality
annotations.definition: A monadic quality of continuant that exists at the cellular level of organisation.

annotations.label: cellular potency
annotations.definition: A cellular quality that arises by virtue of whether the bearer's disposition to differentiate into one or more mature cell types.

annotations.label: self-renewal
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having the ability to go through numerous cycles of cell division while maintaining the undifferentiated state.

annotations.label: totipotent
annotations.definition: A cellular potency that is the capacity to produce differentiated cell types of all three primary germ layers and extraembryonic cell types.

annotations.label: unipotent
annotations.definition: A cellular potency that is the capacity to produce only one differentiated cell type.
annotations.comment: Unipotent cells have the quality of self-renewal which distinguishes them from non-stem cells.

annotations.label: oligopotent
annotations.definition: A cellular potency that is the capacity to form multiple differentiated cell types of a specific lineage and lack self renewing capacity.
annotations.comment: Less potent than multipotent, often thought of as precursor or progenitor cell status.

annotations.label: multipotent
annotations.definition: A cellular potency that is the capacity to form multiple differentiated cell types.

annotations.label: pluripotent
annotations.definition: A cellular potency that is the capacity to produce differentiated cell types of all three primary germ layers but not extraembryonic cell types.

annotations.label: nucleate quality
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's number of nuclei.

annotations.label: anucleate
annotations.definition: A nucleate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having no nucleus.

annotations.label: binucleate
annotations.definition: A nucleate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two nuclei.

annotations.label: mononucleate
annotations.definition: A nucleate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having one nucleus.

annotations.label: ciliated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having one or more cilia as a part.

annotations.label: spindle-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a long tapered rod.

annotations.label: striated
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by narrow lines or grooves, usually parallel.

annotations.label: structured
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having distinct structure.

annotations.label: unstructured
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking distinct structure.

annotations.label: angular velocity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the rate of the bearer's angular movement about an axis; the angle rotated in a given time.

annotations.label: catalytic activity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a catalyst by virtue of the amount of the catalyst's action.

annotations.label: morbidity
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a population by virtue of the proportion of its members that are ill at a given time.

annotations.label: regular duration
annotations.definition: A duration which has regular start and/or end times.

annotations.label: irregular duration
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's duration which has irregular start and/or end times.

annotations.label: lethargic
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being deficient in alertness or activity.

annotations.label: sharp
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's terminating in a point or edge.

annotations.label: buoyancy
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to rise or float in a fluid medium such as water or air.

annotations.label: alive
annotations.definition: A viability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's condition before death.

annotations.label: dead
annotations.definition: A viability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the cessation of the bearer's life.

annotations.label: refractile
annotations.definition: A reflectivity quality inhering in the bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to refract light.

annotations.label: violet
annotations.definition: A color hue with very low wavelength of that portion of the visible spectrum lying between reddish blue or bluish purple, evoked in the human observer by radiant energy with wavelengths of approximately 420 to 380 nanometers.

annotations.label: rosy
annotations.definition: A color hue consisting of red hue and yellow hue and high brightness.

annotations.label: maroon
annotations.definition: A color consisting of purple and brown hue.

annotations.label: compound acidity
annotations.definition: A concentration quality inhering in compound by virtue of the bearer's tendency to act as a hydron donor.

annotations.label: medium acidity
annotations.definition: A concentration quality inhering in a medium by virtue of the bearer's tendency to hydronate a specific reference base.

annotations.label: acidic
annotations.definition: An medium acidity quality inhering in a solution by virtue of the bearer's a high concentration of H+ ions.

annotations.label: alkaline
annotations.definition: An medium acidity quality inhering in a solution by virtue of the bearer's a low concentration of H+ ions.

annotations.label: adhesivity
annotations.definition: An emergent molecular quality that arises by virtue of the bearer's disposition to exhibit molecular attraction to another entity in contact.
annotations.comment: The definition is general enough to cover adhesion arising from different kinds of chemical bonding/forces, although for PATO this term will most commonly used for cellular adhesion. See also GO:0031589 "cell-substrate adhesion".

annotations.label: decayed
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's decomposition into component parts.

annotations.label: growth quality of occurrent
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process which describes the growth of an organism, structure, or group of organisms.

annotations.label: reproductive quality
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to produce new life or offspring.

annotations.label: attachment quality
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having connection or association with another entity.

annotations.label: sessile (sensu botany)
annotations.definition: An attachment quality inhering in a flower by virtue of the bearer's lacking a stalk, as in flowers or leaves that grow directly from the stem.

annotations.label: sessile (sensu zoology)
annotations.definition: A attachment quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's inability to move about.

annotations.label: pedicellate
annotations.definition: A attachment quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or being supported by a pedicel or pedicle.

annotations.label: tonicity
annotations.definition: A contractile quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's elastic tension that facilitate response to stimuli.
annotations.comment: TODO: we need a clearer differentium between this and contractility - or merge?

annotations.label: homophilic
annotations.definition: A cellular adhesivity quality inhering in a molecule in one cell by virtue of the bearer's attachment to an identical molecule in an adjacent cell.

annotations.label: heterophilic
annotations.definition: A cellular adhesivity quality inhering in a molecule in one cell by virtue of the bearer's attachment to an nonidentical molecule in an adjacent cell.

annotations.label: wholeness
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer includes all its components.

annotations.label: broken
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's components no longer being in a single contiguous unit.

annotations.label: disassembled
annotations.definition: A wholeness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being taken apart into its constituent parts.

annotations.label: whole
annotations.definition: A wholeness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's including all its components.

annotations.label: calcified
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's being encrusted or impregnated with calcium carbonate (CHEBI:3311).

annotations.label: ossified
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's being hardened by the deposition of calcium into bone.

annotations.label: cartilaginous
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's cartilage quantities.
annotations.comment: Cartilage is a type of dense connective tissue. It is composed of specialized cells called chondrocytes (CL:0000138) that produce a large amount of extracellular matrix composed of collagen fibers, abundant ground substance rich in proteoglycan, and elastin fibers.

annotations.label: edematous
annotations.definition: A structural quality which is held by a bearer when the latter exhibits an excessive accumulation of extracellular fluid.

annotations.label: detached from
annotations.definition: An attachment quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking connection or association with another entity.

annotations.label: sensitivity to oxygen
annotations.definition: A sensitivity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's dependence on oxygen.

annotations.label: aerobic
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's dependence on oxygen.

annotations.label: anaerobic
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's independence on oxygen.

annotations.label: sensitivity of a process
annotations.definition: A quality of a process inhering in bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to respond to stimulation.

annotations.label: sensitivity of a process to oxygen
annotations.definition: A sensitivity of a process quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's unfolding in a location with or without oxygen.

annotations.label: aerobic (for occurrence)
annotations.definition: A sensitivity of a process inhering in a biological process by virtue of the process unfolding in a location with oxygen.

annotations.label: anaerobic (for occurrence)
annotations.definition: A sensitivity of a process inhering in a biological process by virtue of the process unfolding in a location without oxygen.

annotations.label: surface tension
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a liquid by virtue of the bearer's ability to attraction of molecules at its surface as a result of unbalanced molecular cohesive forces.

annotations.label: membrane potential
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a cell's plasma membrane by virtue of the electrical potential difference across it.

annotations.label: action potential
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a cell's plasma membrane by virtue of a brief fluctuation in the bearer's potential caused by the rapid opening and closing of voltage-gated ion channels.

annotations.label: electric potential
annotations.definition: A quality that is equal to the potential energy per unit charge associated with a static (time-invariant) electric field, also called the electrostatic potential.

annotations.label: monstrous
annotations.definition: A malformed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's all-encompassing, grotesque morphological defects.

annotations.label: curved rostral
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved towards the front of an organism.

annotations.label: curved caudal
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved towards the part of the body from which the tail arises.

annotations.label: curved dorsal
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved towards the back or upper surface of an organism.

annotations.label: curved ventral
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved towards the abdomen of an organism.

annotations.label: decreased depth
annotations.definition: A depth which is relatively low.

annotations.label: duplicated
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being present in two copies.

annotations.label: anteverted
annotations.definition: An oriented quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's axis being positioned forward.

annotations.label: increased position
annotations.definition: A position which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased position
annotations.definition: A positional which is relatively low.

annotations.label: retracted
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being drawn in or pulled back from any given point.

annotations.label: collapsed
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being broken down; caved in.

annotations.label: spongy
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a sponge in elasticity, absorbency, or porousness.

annotations.label: sloped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an oblique or slanted direction.

annotations.label: prominent
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's standing out or projecting beyond a surface or line.

annotations.label: aplastic
annotations.definition: Absence of an organ, tissue or cell due to failure to develop from a primordium or precursor cell.
annotations.comment: With aplasia, the primordium or precursor develops, but does not proceed in its development to a mature structure

annotations.label: recent
annotations.definition: A quality of a process which occurs near to or not long before the present.

annotations.label: condensed
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being thicker or more closely packed together; pressed tightly together.

annotations.label: chronological age
annotations.definition: An age quality that exists by virtue of the time (years and months) that the bearer has existed.

annotations.label: senescent
annotations.definition: A time quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's growing old; aging.

annotations.label: cellular motility
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a cell by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits the ability to move spontaneously.
annotations.comment: Term should be obsoleted and the GO term cellular motility should be used instead.

annotations.label: heterochronic growth
annotations.definition: A growth quality of a process inhering in an organism, structure, or group of organisms by virtue of the bearer's reduced or increased growth.

annotations.label: neotenous growth
annotations.definition: A paedomorphic growth quality which is due to a reduced rate.

annotations.label: growth rate
annotations.definition: The rate or speed of the change in size of an individual entity or change in number of a population.

annotations.label: hypertrophic growth
annotations.comment: This term will be obsoleted; Use GO:growth and PATO:rate.

annotations.label: hypotrophic growth
annotations.comment: This term will be obsoleted; Use GO:growth and PATO:rate.

annotations.label: notched
annotations.definition: A concave quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape being a V-shaped cut.

annotations.label: brood quality
annotations.definition: A reproductive quality inhering in the young of an animal by virtue of the bearer's being cared for at one time.

annotations.label: brood viability
annotations.definition: A reproductive quality inhering in the young of an animal by virtue of the bearer's disposition to survive and develop normally.

annotations.label: spherical
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a ball (a sphere whose equatorial diameter is equal to the polar diameter).

annotations.label: tapered
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being gradually narrower or thinner toward one end.

annotations.label: immature
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking complete growth, differentiation, or development.

annotations.label: complexity
annotations.definition: A monadic quality of continuant inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being intricate and compounded.
annotations.comment: TODO: obsolete this.

annotations.label: simple
annotations.definition: A complexity quality in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having few parts or being complicated or compound.

annotations.label: complex
annotations.definition: A complexity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being intricate and compounded.

annotations.label: separated from
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not being in contact with another entity, or of no longer being connected or joined with another entity.

annotations.label: diastatic
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's separation of normally joined parts, such as the separation of adjacent bones without fracture or of certain abdominal muscles during pregnancy.

annotations.label: disrupted
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being interrupted of its normal course.

annotations.label: functionality
annotations.definition: A quality of a single physical entity that arises by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits the ability to perform a regular function(s).

annotations.label: functional
annotations.definition: A functionality quality held by the bearer when the latter is able to perform a regular function(s).

annotations.label: non-functional
annotations.definition: A disfunctional quality held by the bearer when the latter is unable to perform a regular function(s).

annotations.label: punctate
annotations.definition: A pattern inhering in a surface by virtue of the bearer's being marked by the presence of dots, punctures, points or pits.

annotations.label: delaminated
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking some outer layer.

annotations.label: variable color
annotations.definition: A color quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being uneven in color.

annotations.label: focus
annotations.definition: An optical quality where light rays originating from a point on the object converge.

annotations.label: focused
annotations.definition: An optical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having focus.

annotations.label: blurry
annotations.definition: An optical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking focus.

annotations.label: sound quality
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of vibration, as perceived by the sense of hearing.

annotations.label: sound frequency
annotations.definition: The frequency of transmitted vibrations.

annotations.label: sound amplitude
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a sound wave by virtue of the maximum magnitude (value without regard to sign) of its disturbance.

annotations.label: sound speed
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in sound by virtue of the bearer's travelling speed in a given medium under specified conditions.

annotations.label: sound wavelength
annotations.definition: A physical quality which is equal to the quotient of the speed of a sound wave divided by its wavelength.

annotations.label: intrasonic
annotations.definition: A sound frequency in the range of 0-20 Hz.

annotations.label: sonic
annotations.definition: A sound frequency in the range of 20 to 20.000 Hz.

annotations.label: ultrasonic
annotations.definition: A sound frequency in the range of 20 000 + Hz.

annotations.label: quiet
annotations.definition: A sound amplitude which is relatively low.

annotations.label: loud
annotations.definition: A sound amplitude which is relatively high.

annotations.label: cellular spatiotemporal quality
annotations.definition: A spatiotemporal quality inhering in a cell.

annotations.label: behavioral quality of a process
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's behavior.

annotations.label: cellular adhesivity
annotations.definition: A cellular quality that exists by virtue of the disposition of the cell to adhere to other cells and molecules, through the emergent action of the molecular parts of the cell surface.

annotations.label: mono-colored
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having one hue.

annotations.label: multi-colored
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's having two or more hues or degrees of saturation.

annotations.label: motley
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's having 3 or more hues or degrees of saturations.

annotations.label: dappled
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's being marked with small spots, patches, or specks of hue or degree of saturation differing from that of the background.

annotations.label: solubility
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of the bearer's disposition to dissolve in a liquid.

annotations.label: soluble in
annotations.definition: A solubility quality inhering in a substance by virtue of the bearer's disposition to dissolve in a liquid.

annotations.label: insoluble in
annotations.definition: A solubility quality inhering in a substance by virtue of the bearer's inability of a substance to dissolve in a liquid.

annotations.label: amylose composition
annotations.definition: A compositional quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's quantities or relative ratios of amylose of the inhering entity.

annotations.label: glutinous
annotations.definition: An amylose composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's having negligible or no amylose content.

annotations.label: non-glutinous
annotations.definition: An amylose composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's having high amount of amylose content.

annotations.label: delayed phase
annotations.definition: A phase which starts later than natural start point.

annotations.label: flexibility
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to being turned, bowed, or twisted without breaking.

annotations.label: flexible
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability of being turned, bowed, or twisted without breaking.

annotations.label: inflexible
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's inability of being turned, bowed, or twisted without breaking.

annotations.label: quality of a solid
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting the physical characteristics of an entity characterized by particles arranged such that their shape and volume are relatively stable.

annotations.label: quality of a gas
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting the physical characteristics of an entity consisting of particles that have neither a defined volume nor defined shape.

annotations.label: quality of a liquid
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in an entity exhibiting the physical characteristics of an amorphous (non-crystalline) form of matter between a gas and a solid that has a definite volume, but no definite shape.

annotations.label: increased sensitivity toward
annotations.definition: A sensitivity toward an external stimulus which is higher than normal/average.

annotations.label: decreased sensitivity toward
annotations.definition: A sensitivity toward an external stimulus which is lower than normal/average.

annotations.label: increased sensitivity of a process
annotations.definition: A sensitivity of a process which is higher than normal or average.

annotations.label: decreased sensitivity of a process
annotations.definition: A sensitivity of a process which is lower than normal or average.

annotations.label: increased sensitivity of a process to oxygen
annotations.definition: A sensitivity of occurrent to oxygen which is higher than normal or average.

synonyms.text: high sensitivity of occurrent to oxygen

annotations.label: decreased sensitivity of a process to oxygen
annotations.definition: A sensitivity of a process to oxygen which is lower than normal or average.

synonyms.text: low sensitivity of occurrent to oxygen

annotations.label: has number of
annotations.definition: The number of parts of a particular type that the bearer entity has. This is a relational quality, and thus holds between two entities: the bearer of the quality, and the type of parts.

synonyms.text: extra or missing physical or functional parts
has or lacks parts of type

annotations.label: lacking processual parts
annotations.definition: A quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking a processual part as specified by the additional entity.

annotations.label: having extra function
annotations.definition: A functionality quality which is held by the bearer when the latter is able to perform additional or different function(s).

annotations.label: having extra processual parts
annotations.definition: A quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having additional processual parts.

annotations.label: decreased mass
annotations.definition: A mass which is lower than normal or average.

annotations.label: increased mass
annotations.definition: A mass which is higher than normal or average.

annotations.label: extra or missing processual parts
annotations.definition: A quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's processual parts.

annotations.label: distributed
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being spread out or scattered about or divided up.

annotations.label: undistributed
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not being spread out or scattered about or divided up.

annotations.label: having decreased processual parts
annotations.definition: A quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having fewer processual parts.

annotations.label: dilated
annotations.definition: A size quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being made wider or larger in all dimensions.

annotations.label: isometrical
annotations.definition: A symmetry quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having equal dimensions or measurements.

annotations.label: flow rate

annotations.label: decreased pressure
annotations.definition: A pressure which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased pressure
annotations.definition: A pressure which is relatively high.

annotations.label: increased permeability
annotations.definition: A permeability which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased permeability
annotations.definition: A permeability which is relatively low.

annotations.label: contractility
annotations.definition: A physical quality that is the ability to contract or shrink.

annotations.label: increased contractility
annotations.definition: A contractility which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased contractility
annotations.definition: A contractility which is relatively low.

annotations.label: decreased variability
annotations.definition: A variability which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased variability
annotations.definition: A variability which is relatively high.

annotations.label: conductivity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to transmit of an entity through a medium.
annotations.comment: Examples could be heat or electricity or sound.

annotations.label: variability of rate
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits rate variation or change.

annotations.label: increased variability of rate
annotations.definition: A variability of rate which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased variability of rate
annotations.definition: A variability of rate which is relatively low.

annotations.label: overlap with
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's coinciding spatially, partially or wholly with another entity.

annotations.label: curvature
annotations.definition: A surface shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a degree of bending.

annotations.label: increased curvature
annotations.definition: A curvature which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased curvature
annotations.definition: A curvature which is relatively low.

annotations.label: arched
annotations.definition: A concave quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's forming or resembling an arch.

annotations.label: depth
annotations.definition: A 1-D extent quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's downward or backward or inward dimension.

annotations.label: increased depth
annotations.definition: A depth quality which is relatively high.

annotations.label: everted
annotations.definition: An oriented quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being turned outward in placement.

annotations.label: protruding
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's extending out above or beyond a surface or boundary.

annotations.label: rotated
annotations.definition: An oriented quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being relocated around an axis.

annotations.label: posteriorly rotated
annotations.definition: A rotation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of being rotated towards the rear of organism.

annotations.label: anteriorly rotated
annotations.definition: A rotation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of being rotated towards the front of an organism.

annotations.label: distended
annotations.definition: A size quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being abnormally expanded or increased in size.

annotations.label: increased life span
annotations.definition: A life span which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased life span
annotations.definition: A life span which is relatively low.

annotations.label: disheveled
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being in disarray; extremely disorderly.

annotations.label: greasy
annotations.definition: A coating which is oily and slippery.

annotations.label: matted
annotations.definition: A tangled quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being not possible to unravel.

annotations.label: patchy
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by, consisting of, or diversified with patches.

annotations.label: sparse
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being scattered; spread irregularly, and at a distance from each other.

annotations.label: variability of color
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits color variation or change.

annotations.label: increased variability of color
annotations.definition: A variability of color which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased variability of color
annotations.definition: A variability of color which is relatively low.

annotations.label: invariant color
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking or not exhibiting variation of color.

annotations.label: variant color
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or exhibiting variation of colour.

annotations.label: deformed
annotations.definition: A morphological quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being distorted in form.

annotations.label: increased tonicity
annotations.definition: A tonicity which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased tonicity
annotations.definition: A tonicity which is relatively low.

annotations.label: turgor
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a cell or blood vessel or capillary by virtue of the bearer's being in a rigid state of fullness resulting from pressure of the contents against the wall or membrane.

annotations.label: decreased turgor
annotations.definition: A turgor which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased turgor
annotations.definition: A turgor which is relatively high.

annotations.label: atrophied
annotations.definition: A size quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of a part or parts of the bearer's being decreased in size due to reduction in tissue mass through wasting.

annotations.label: decreased functionality
annotations.definition: A functionality quality held by the bearer when the latter exhibits decreased ability to perform a regular function(s).

annotations.label: increased functionality
annotations.definition: A functional quality held by the bearer when the latter exhibits increased ability to perform a regular function(s).

annotations.label: sufficiency
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer has enough functionality.
annotations.comment: This term and children should probably be obsoleted.

annotations.label: sufficient
annotations.definition: A wholeness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's has enough functionality.

annotations.label: insufficient
annotations.definition: A wholeness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacks enough functionality.

annotations.label: aggregated
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being gathered or tending to gather into a mass or whole.

annotations.label: dispersed
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being distributed or spread over a considerable extent.

annotations.label: anterior to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the front of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: posterior to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the rear of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: necessity (continuant)
annotations.definition: A quality of a single physical entity inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer is essential or indispensable.

annotations.label: unnecessary (continuant)

annotations.label: necessity of occurrent

annotations.label: unnecessary (occurrent)

annotations.label: stubby

annotations.label: protruding out of
annotations.definition: A quality inhering into a bearer by virtue of the bearer's extending out above or beyond its surface or boundary and outwards in relation to the physical space occupied by another entity.

annotations.label: circumference

annotations.label: curved lateral

annotations.label: increased resistance to

annotations.label: decreased resistance to

annotations.label: misaligned with
annotations.definition: An alignment quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being in a improper spatial positioning with respect to an additional entity.

annotations.definition: A osmolarity which is relatively high.

annotations.definition: A susceptibility toward an external stimulus which is lower than normal/average.

annotations.definition: A distribution which is relatively high.

annotations.definition: A distribution which is relatively low.

annotations.label: molar mass

annotations.label: magnetism
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to exert an attractive or repulsive force on other entities.

annotations.definition: A magnetism which is relatively low.

annotations.definition: An elevation which is relatively high.

annotations.definition: An elevation which is relatively low.

annotations.comment: This refers to the disposition of the bearer.

annotations.label: increased viscosity
annotations.definition: A viscosity which relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased viscosity
annotations.definition: A viscosity which relatively low.

annotations.label: increased fecundity

annotations.definition: A fecundity which is relatively low.

annotations.comment: Radiation quality is often expressed in terms of the linear energy transfer of the ionising particles that deliver the absorbed dose, but this is an over simplification. {xref="DOI:10.2307/3570787"}

annotations.label: activity (of a radionuclide)

annotations.comment: See https://github.com/pato-ontology/pato/issues/490

annotations.label: decreased radioactivity
annotations.definition: A radioactivity which is relatively high.

annotations.label: irradiated

annotations.comment: This may refer to incident external irradiation or internal radiation from an ingested or otherwise internal source.

annotations.label: predisplaced growth

annotations.label: hypermorphic growth

annotations.definition: An intensity which is relatively low.

annotations.definition: A coiling which is relatively low.

annotations.definition: A rhythm quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking rhythm lacking pattern.

annotations.label: tangled
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being entwined and difficult to unravel.

annotations.label: constricted
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being drawn together, compressed or squeezed physically.

annotations.label: asymmetrically curved
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved asymmetrically.

annotations.label: abrased
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of a portion of the bearer's surface being scraped away.

annotations.label: scarred
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being fibrous tissue that replaces normal tissue destroyed by injury or disease.

annotations.label: swollen
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting transient abnormal enlargement, not due to cell proliferation.

annotations.label: dislocated
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being out of its usual or proper place, or position due to it being physically moved from the normal location.

annotations.label: hydrocephalic
annotations.definition: A structural quality which is held by a bearer when the latter exhibits an excessive accumulation of cerebral spinal fluid.

annotations.label: vertical
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being situated at right angles to the horizon.

annotations.label: horizontal
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being in the plane of the horizon.

annotations.label: introverted
annotations.definition: An oriented quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being turned inward upon itself.
annotations.comment: For example, a tubular organ or part.

annotations.label: concave
annotations.definition: A shape quality in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's curving inward.

annotations.label: partially paralysed
annotations.definition: A functionality quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of being not completely paralysed.

annotations.label: increased coordination
annotations.definition: A coordination which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased coordination
annotations.definition: A coordination which is relatively low.

annotations.label: fasciculated
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's forming a bundle of aligned anatomical fibers, as of muscle or nerve.

annotations.label: lack of fertility in offspring
annotations.definition: A fertility quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to make its offspring sterile.

annotations.label: chronic
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having slow progressive course of indefinite duration.

annotations.label: spheroid
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a quadric surface in three dimensions obtained by rotating an ellipse about one of its principal axes. Includes spheres and oblate/prolate spheroids.

annotations.label: prolate
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a polar diameter longer than its equatorial diameter.

annotations.label: rectangular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of it being a quadrilateral polygon in which all four angles are right angles.

annotations.label: autofluorescence
annotations.definition: A fluorescence quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting fluorescence which is self-induced.

annotations.label: pathological
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being abnormal and having a destructive effect on living tissue.

annotations.label: crescent-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a circular disk by virtue of the bearer's having a segment of another circle removed from its edge, so that what remains is a shape enclosed by two circular arcs of different diameters which intersect at two points (usually in such a manner that the enclosed shape does not include the center of the original circle).

annotations.label: reniform
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having the shape of a kidney.

annotations.label: cuboid
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resemblance to a cube (a 3-D shape with a square cross section).

annotations.label: cylindrical
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a consistently-sized round cross section.

annotations.label: discoid
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being cylindrical, in which the height is less than the diameter.

annotations.label: triangular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having three angles.

annotations.label: dumbbell-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two parts, roughly spherical and of equal size, connected by a bar.

annotations.label: lanceolate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped like a lance-head, considerably longer than wide, tapering towards the tip from below the middle; attached at the broad end.

annotations.label: sigmoid
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting of two curves, in opposite directions. S-shaped.

annotations.label: U-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of the letter U.

annotations.label: caudate
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a tail or tail-like projection.

annotations.label: arrow-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's forming two equal obtuse triangles with a short side in common.
annotations.comment: Gradually enlarged at the base, like the head of an arrow.

annotations.label: limaciform
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a slug.

annotations.label: clavate
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gradually becoming thicker towards the end.

annotations.label: hydrophobicity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue the bearer's disposition to being water-repellent; tending to repel and not absorb water.

annotations.label: hydrophobic
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking affinity for water; tending to repel and not absorb water; tending not to dissolve in or mix with or be wetted by water.

annotations.label: hydrophilicity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue the bearer's disposition to having an affinity for water; it is readily absorbing or dissolving in water.

annotations.label: hydrophilic
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to having a strong affinity for water; tending to dissolve in, mix with, or be wetted by water.

annotations.label: miscibility
annotations.definition: A quality of a liquid inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to mix with (dissolve in) another liquid.

annotations.label: scalloped
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having the border, edge, or outline cut into a series of segments of circles resembling a scallop-shell.

annotations.label: tripartite
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being divided into three branches.

annotations.label: ovate
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a continuous convex surface with an axis of symmetry and one axis longer than the other; egg-shaped.

annotations.label: decreased odor
annotations.definition: An odor quality of having decreased odor.

annotations.label: increased odor
annotations.definition: An odor quality of having increased odor.

annotations.label: phenotypic sex
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's physical expression of sexual characteristics.

annotations.label: mating type
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population that undergo sexual reproduction.

annotations.label: unlumenized
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking of a three dimensional space surrounded by one or more anatomical structures and containing one or more anatomical substances.

annotations.label: lumenized
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a three dimensional space surrounded by one or more anatomical structures and containing one or more anatomical substances.
annotations.comment: See GO term: GO:0031974 membrane-enclosed lumen [DEF:"The enclosed volume within a sealed membrane or between two sealed membranes"].

annotations.label: increased circumference
annotations.definition: A circumference which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased circumference
annotations.definition: A circumference which is relatively low.

annotations.label: front
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's direction that is similar to the direction of an object to the south when it faces north.

annotations.label: back
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's direction that is similar to the direction of an object to the north when it faces south.

annotations.label: unidirectional
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having one direction only.

annotations.label: bi-directional
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two directions.

annotations.label: circling direction
annotations.definition: A directional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's direction approximating the shape of a circle.

annotations.label: has normal numbers of parts of type
annotations.definition: The bearer of this quality has_part = n, where n is the normal amount for a comparable organism. Note that the bearer of the quality is the whole, not the part.

annotations.label: movement quality
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's participation in movement.

annotations.label: botryoidal
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being like the surface of a compact mass of grapes.

annotations.label: multinucleate
annotations.definition: A nucleate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having more than one nucleus.

annotations.label: trinucleate
annotations.definition: A nucleate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having three nuclei.

annotations.label: folded
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's one part being layered over another connected part.

annotations.label: circling
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting movement in a circular course.

annotations.label: physiological state
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the state of bearer's mechanical, physical, and biochemical processes.

annotations.label: ictal
annotations.definition: A physiological state which is characterized by periods of high-frequency high amplitude electrical activity in neuronal tissue.

annotations.label: non-ictal
annotations.definition: A physiological state which is characterized by normal electrical activity in neuronal tissue.

annotations.label: anterodorsal to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the front and upper surface of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: posterodorsal to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the rear and upper surface of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: anteroventral to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the front and abdomen of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: posteroventral to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the rear and abdomen of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: mislocalised dorsally
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards the back or upper surface of an organism.

annotations.label: mislocalised ventrally
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards the abdomen of an organism.

annotations.label: mislocalised anteriorly
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards the front of an organism.

annotations.label: mislocalised posteriorly
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards the rear of an organism.

annotations.label: mislocalised laterally
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards the side of an organism.

annotations.label: mislocalised medially
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards the middle of an organism.

annotations.label: surface feature shape
annotations.definition: A surface shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape of features present on its surface or outer shell.
annotations.comment: This quality either inheres directly in the surface, or in the entity that has the surface. For example, to say that a particular fly wing is curved is shorthand for saying the wing has a surface which is curved.

annotations.label: increased fluorescence
annotations.definition: A fluorescence which is higher than normal.

annotations.label: decreased fluorescence
annotations.definition: A fluorescence which is lower than normal.

annotations.label: blistered
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of a local accumulation of fluid underneath the surface of the bearer.

annotations.label: variability of shape
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits shape variation or change.

annotations.label: variant shape
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue the bearer's having or exhibiting variation its shape.

annotations.label: invariant shape
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking or not exhibiting variation of shape.

annotations.label: alternate placement
annotations.definition: A pattern quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being placed alternately one above the other.

annotations.label: opposite
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned on opposite sides on the same plane.

annotations.label: orbicular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being perfectly circular.

annotations.label: obtuse
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being roundish, a little inclining to be oblong.

annotations.label: obovate
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being egg-shaped and flat, with the narrow end attached to the base.

annotations.label: spatulate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being oblong, with the lower end very much attenuated.

annotations.label: rhomboid
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being oval and a little angular in the middle.

annotations.label: gigantic
annotations.definition: A size quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being many folds greater relative to the normal average.

annotations.label: yellow green
annotations.definition: A color consisting of yellow and green hues.

annotations.label: brown green
annotations.definition: A color consisting of brown and green hues.

annotations.label: lilac
annotations.definition: A purple color which has low saturation and low brightness.

annotations.label: yellow orange
annotations.definition: A color consisting of yellow and orange hue.

annotations.label: ochre
annotations.definition: A moderate yellow-orange to orange color.

annotations.label: banded
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's exhibiting transverse stripes of one hue or degree of saturation crossing another.

annotations.label: netted
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's being covered with reticulated lines of a different hue or degree of saturation.

annotations.label: furrowed
annotations.definition: A grooved texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by two or more parallel channels.

annotations.label: pointleted
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's terminating abruptly in a little point.

annotations.label: blunt
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's terminating gradually in a rounded end.

annotations.label: whorled
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being in opposition round a common axis.

annotations.label: distichous
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being arranged in two opposite rows (and hence in the same plane).

annotations.label: decussate
annotations.definition: A pattern quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting of paired parts that alternately cross each other.

annotations.label: subulate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being linear, very narrow, tapering to a very fine point from a narrow base.

annotations.label: cuneate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being narrowly triangular, wider at the apex and tapering toward the base.

annotations.label: variability of size
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits size variation or change.

annotations.label: decreased variability of size
annotations.definition: A variability of size which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased variability of size
annotations.definition: A variability of size which is relatively high.

annotations.label: defasciculated
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's in which bundles of aligned anatomical fibers have become separated.

annotations.label: interdigitated
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's parts or projections being interlocked; for example, the fingers of two hands that are clasped.

annotations.label: in contact with
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's touching another entity.

annotations.label: involute
annotations.definition: A curled quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's edges of its surface being rolled inwards spirally on each side.

annotations.label: revolute
annotations.definition: A curled quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's edges of its surface being rolled backwards spirally on each side.

annotations.label: circinate
annotations.definition: A curled quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's edges of its surface being rolled spirally downwards.

annotations.label: reclinate
annotations.definition: A curled quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's edges of its surface being bent down upon their stalk.

annotations.label: convolute
annotations.definition: A curled quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's one edge of its surface being wholly rolled up in another.

annotations.label: actinodromous
annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having three or more primary branches diverging radially from a single point.

annotations.label: parallelodromous
annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two or more parallel primary branches originate beside each other and converge apically.

annotations.label: craspedodromous
annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having secondary branches terminating at the margin.

annotations.label: brochidodromous
annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having secondary branches joined together in a series of prominent arches.

annotations.label: cladodromous
