annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having secondary branches freely branching toward the margin.

annotations.label: reticulodromous
annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having secondary branches branching into a reticulum toward the margin.

annotations.label: cuspidate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being tapering gradually into a rigid point.

annotations.label: retuse
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's terminating in a round end, the centre of which is depressed.

annotations.label: unserrated
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking sharp straight-edged teeth pointing to the apex.

annotations.label: serration
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or lacking sharp straight-edged teeth pointing to the apex.

annotations.label: angular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having at least one salient angle on the margin.

annotations.label: cut
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being regularly divided by deep incisions.

annotations.label: lobed
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being partly divided into a determinate number of regions.

annotations.label: digitate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having distinct parts arising from a common point or center.

annotations.label: auriculate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a shape resembling an ear.

annotations.label: attenuate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gradually tapering to a slender point.

annotations.label: perfoliate
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's two basal lobes being united together.

annotations.label: decurrent
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being prolonged below the point of insertion.

annotations.label: frozen
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being kept below its freezing point.

annotations.label: dissolved
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's passing into solution.

annotations.label: saccular
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a three dimensional cavity with a narrow or no opening, and often containing an anatomical substance.

annotations.label: quadrangular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having four angles and four sides.

annotations.label: sinuous
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having many curves and turns.

annotations.label: duration of disease
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process inhering in a disease by virtue of the bearer's duration of an impairment of health or of a condition of abnormal functioning.
annotations.comment: MAH: I think this term should be obsoleted. Is it used?

annotations.label: immortal
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to being capable of indefinite growth or division.

annotations.label: cellularity
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting cells.

annotations.label: multicellular
annotations.definition: A cellularity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting of more than one cell.

annotations.label: unicellular
annotations.definition: A cellularity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting of exactly one cell.

annotations.label: organismal quality
annotations.definition: A quality that inheres in an entire organism or part of an organism.

annotations.label: decreased amount
annotations.definition: An amount which is relatively low.

synonyms.text: present in fewer numbers in organism

annotations.label: conspicuousness
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its visibility.
annotations.comment: This term was created as a grouping term for the 2 terms "conspicuous" and "inconspicuous". However, it is not clear if these terms should even remain in PATO.

annotations.label: lacks parts or has fewer parts of type
annotations.definition: The bearer of this quality has_part < n of the indicated entity type, where n is the normal amount for a comparable organism. Note that the bearer of the quality is the whole, not the part. Formally: If a bearer entity e has fewer parts of type X at time t, then the number of instances x of X at t such that x part_of e is < n, where n is either the normal number for comparable entities, or n is stated explicitly. This case includes the limit case, where the bearer lacks all parts of the specified type.

annotations.label: lacks all parts of type
annotations.definition: A quality of physical entities inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking a physical part as specified by the additional entity.
annotations.comment: Example: [E=organism Q=lacks_all_parts_of_type E2=Wing] - applies to an organism. A relational quality in which the bearer entity has no parts of the specified type. The bearer of this quality has_part = 0 of the indicated entity type, where a comparable organism usually has at least 1 part of the same type. Note that the bearer of the quality is the whole, not the part. Formally: If a bearer entity e lacks all parts of type X at time t, then there exists no instances x of X at t such that x part_of e that has no wings, where wings are normally present in that organism type. In OWL this is equivalent to a restriction on the OBO_REL:has_part relation with cardinality=0, i.e has_part 0 E2.

synonyms.text: lacks all physical parts of type

annotations.label: has fewer parts of type
annotations.definition: The bearer of this quality has_part < n AND has_part > 0 of the indicated entity type, where n is the normal amount for a comparable organism. Note that the bearer of the quality is the whole, not the part. Formally: If a bearer entity e has fewer parts of type X at time t, then the number of instances x of X at t such that x part_of e is < n, where n is either the normal number for comparable entities, or n is stated explicitly.
annotations.comment: Example: [E=hand Q=has_fewer_parts_of_type E2=digit] - applies to an organism that has no less fingers than is normal for organisms of that type.

synonyms.text: has fewer physical parts of type

annotations.label: has extra parts of type
annotations.definition: The bearer of this quality has_part > n of the indicated entity type, where n is the normal amount for a comparable organism. Note that the bearer of the quality is the whole, not the part.
annotations.comment: In polydactyly, the bearer of the quality is the hand, and the entity type being counted is 'finger'. In EQ syntax, E=hand, Q=<this> E2=finger.

annotations.label: population quality
annotations.definition: A quality that inheres in an entire population or part of a population.

annotations.label: concavity
annotations.definition: Surface shape that refers to the inward or outward curvature of the surface.

annotations.label: 2-D shape
annotations.definition: A shape that inheres in a 2 dimensional entity, such as a cross section or projection of a 3 dimensional entity.

annotations.label: convex 3-D shape
annotations.definition: A complete three dimensional shape in which for every line connecting pair of points on the object is within the object. Or: a shape lacking cavities. Contrast: concave.
annotations.comment: Use this term or an is_a child of this term when the entire shape of the object is known.

annotations.label: concave 3-D shape
annotations.definition: A complete three dimensional shape in which there is a line connecting pair of points on the object that lies outside the object. Or: a shape with cavities. Contrast: concave.

annotations.label: branchiness
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the degree to which there are subdivisions or offshoots in a bearer entity.

annotations.label: looseness
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's bing full of small openings or gaps.

annotations.label: neoplastic
annotations.definition: A structural quality which is held by a bearer when the latter's disposition the presence of abnormally proliferating masses of cells.

annotations.label: coating
annotations.definition: A texture quality that exists through a liquid cover on the surface of the bearer.

annotations.label: fasciculation
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of whether the bearer forms a bundle of anatomical fibers, as of muscle or nerve.

annotations.label: structure, cavities
annotations.definition: A structural quality that inheres in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing hollow areas.

annotations.label: aplastic growth
annotations.definition: A growth quality of occurrent in which the growth of an organism, structure or group of organisms does not occur.

annotations.label: pale pink
annotations.definition: Pink color having high brightness and low saturation.

annotations.label: conical
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a cone (a 3-D shape with a round cross section that tapers).

annotations.label: villiform
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a minute projection (villus).

annotations.label: apical-basal polarity
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's location of features or characteristics along an apical-basal axis.

annotations.label: anterior-posterior polarity
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's location of features or characteristics along the anterior-posterior axis.

annotations.label: external angle
annotations.definition: A convex angle that is formed by one side of a polygon and a line extended from an adjacent side.

annotations.label: non-progressive
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not increasing over time.

annotations.label: osmolality
annotations.definition: A concentration quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's amount of osmoles of solute per kilogram of solvent.

annotations.label: decreased osmolality
annotations.definition: An osmolality which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased osmolality
annotations.definition: An osmolality which is relatively high.

annotations.label: specificity to
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits the ability of a probe to react precisely with a specific target molecule.

annotations.label: upturned
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved or tilted such that the tip points upwards.

annotations.label: forward scatter
annotations.definition: A reflectivity quality inhering in light by virtue of the bearer's scattering occurring at small angle to the incident beam.

annotations.label: side scatter
annotations.definition: A reflective quality that is the light scattering occurring at wide angle (around 90 degrees) to the incident beam.

annotations.label: undivided
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being unseparated into parts or divisions.

annotations.label: ipsilateral to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being on the same side in relation to another structure.

annotations.label: axial to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located towards the central axis of the organism or an extremity.

annotations.label: degeneration
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer exhibiting deterioration of its structure.

annotations.label: non-degenerate
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer whose structure which does not deteriorate.

annotations.label: biconcave
annotations.definition: A concave quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's curving inward on both sides or surfaces.

annotations.label: biconvex
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being convex on both sides or surface.

annotations.label: anterior-posterior diameter
annotations.definition: A diameter that is along the anterior-posterior axis.

annotations.label: decreased anterior-posterior diameter
annotations.definition: An anterior-posterior diameter quality which is relatively small.

annotations.label: increased anterior-posterior diameter
annotations.definition: An anterior-posterior diameter quality which is relatively large.

annotations.label: trapezoid
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being a quadrilateral with two parallel sides.

annotations.label: dendritic
annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having smaller branches arising from larger branches. Resembling a tree in branching structure.

annotations.label: abaxial to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being away from or on the opposite side of the central axis.

annotations.label: adaxial to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located on the side nearest to the axis of an organ or organism.

annotations.label: mosaicism
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two or more cell populations that differ in genetic makeup. This situation can affect any type of cell, including blood cells, gametes (egg and sperm cells), and skin.

annotations.label: lateral and rotional curvature
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved around an axis and towards the side.

annotations.label: normal amount
annotations.definition: An amount which normal.

synonyms.text: present in normal numbers in organism

annotations.label: increased occurrence
annotations.definition: An occurrence which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased occurrence
annotations.definition: An occurrence which is relatively low.

annotations.label: dorso-ventrally flattened
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being flattened along dorso-ventral axis.

annotations.label: laterally compressed
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being flattened along transverse axis.

annotations.label: increased fragility
annotations.definition: A fragility which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased fragility
annotations.definition: A fragility which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased area
annotations.definition: An area which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased area
annotations.definition: An area which is relatively low.

annotations.label: emmision wavelength
annotations.definition: The specific wavelength of light emitted by a fluorescent molecule, such as a labelled probe, upon absorption of light at the (higher) excitation wavelength.

annotations.label: absorption wavelength
annotations.definition: The specific wavelength of radiation that can be retained by a bearer.

annotations.label: columnar
annotations.definition: A shape inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being elongated and cylindrical.

annotations.label: fenestrated
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's delimited by a surface with holes.

annotations.label: star shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being arranged like rays or radii; radiating from a common center.

annotations.label: crystal configuration
annotations.definition: A solid configuration inhering in a material entity by virtue of its constituent atoms, molecules, or ions being arranged in an orderly repeating pattern extending in all three spatial dimensions.

annotations.label: stratification
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's layered configuration.

annotations.label: stratified
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a layered configuration.

annotations.label: unstratified
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking a layered configuration.

annotations.label: affinity
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that arises from the molecular attraction exerted between two atoms or compounds.

annotations.label: increased affinity
annotations.definition: An affinity which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased affinity
annotations.definition: An affinity which is relatively low.

annotations.label: avidity
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that arises from the synergistic strength of bond affinities between multiple bond interactions.

annotations.label: increased avidity
annotations.definition: An avidity which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased avidity
annotations.definition: An avidity which is relatively low.

annotations.label: movement behavioral quality
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's movement.

annotations.label: stacked
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned with multiple entities of the same type above and below.

annotations.label: hollow
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an empty space or cavity within.

annotations.label: broken into two pieces
annotations.definition: A broken quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being separated into two contiguous wholes.

annotations.label: shattered
annotations.definition: A broken quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being broken into multiple contiguous wholes.

annotations.label: partially broken
annotations.definition: A broken quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being still one contiguous whole but structurally damaged such that in danger of become two or more.

annotations.label: altered number of
annotations.definition: Having extra or fewer parts.

annotations.label: bullet-shaped
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being short and wide and tapered distally.

annotations.label: monocystic
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's body containing one cyst - a formation of a sac-like closed membranous structure not associated with neoplasia.

annotations.label: polycystic
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's body containing more than one cyst - formations of sac-like closed membranous structures not associated with neoplasia.

annotations.label: adenomatous
annotations.definition: A morphology quality inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting the morphological characteristics of a glandular epithelial cell.

annotations.label: subacute
annotations.definition: A quality of a single process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an onset and time course between acute and chronic.

annotations.label: anaplastic
annotations.definition: A morphology quality inhering in a neoplastic cell by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting breakdown of cell-cell interaction maintaining tissue architecture, proliferative dysregulation and bizarre modification to nucleus size and shape.

annotations.label: ballooning
annotations.definition: A volume quality inhering in a degenerating cell by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting an increase in volume associated with cytoplasmic abnormalities.

annotations.label: basophilic
annotations.definition: An affinity inhering in an anatomical structure by virtue of the bearer exhibiting a molecular interaction for basic dyes under specific pH conditions.

annotations.label: neoplastic, non-malignant
annotations.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition not to progress, invade surrounding tissues or metastasize.

annotations.label: neoplastic, malignant
annotations.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition to progress, invade surrounding tissues or metastasize.

annotations.label: neoplastic, metastatic
annotations.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition to spread and invade distant tissues.

annotations.label: differentiated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its stable specialization to a particular cell type.

annotations.label: undifferentiated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of having not yet acquired a special structure of function.

annotations.label: metaplastic
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of having changed from one fully differentiated state into another.

annotations.label: proliferative
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a cell that arises by virtue of whether the bearer exhibits the ability to grow and proliferate in number through cell division.

annotations.label: infiltrative
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's penetrating or permeating another substance or area.

annotations.label: inflamed
annotations.definition: A compositional quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's infiltration by leukocytes, local edema and accumulation of plasma proteins.

annotations.label: hemorrhagic
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting excessive discharge of blood from the blood vessels.

annotations.label: poorly differentiated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its lacking most of the cellular characteristics which would allow it to be fully differentiated.

annotations.label: peripheral
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being at the edge or boundary of a related entity.

annotations.label: papillary
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling small nipple like projection.

annotations.label: caseous
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling cheese.

annotations.label: well differentiated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its having most of the cellular characteristics of the tissue of origin.

annotations.label: moderately well differentiated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its lacking few of the cellular characteristics which would allow it to be fully differentiated.

annotations.label: perforate
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a hole or holes, especially a row or array of small holes.

annotations.label: cribriform
annotations.definition: Perforated like a sieve.

annotations.label: fatty
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing excess lipid.

annotations.label: fibrinoid
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing fibrin.

annotations.label: fibrinopurulent
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in exudate by virtue of the bearer's containing fibrin.

annotations.label: glandular
annotations.definition: A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhbiting the morphological characteristics of a gland.

annotations.label: multiple
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being present in more than two copies.

annotations.label: hydropic
annotations.definition: A structural quality which is held by a bearer when the latter exhibits an excessive accumulation of intracellular fluid.

annotations.label: suppurative
annotations.definition: A quality inhering a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to generate pus.

annotations.label: trabecular
annotations.definition: A spatial pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's structure resembling an irregular meshwork with cross-linking struts.

annotations.label: mixed
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being composed of more than one identifiable entities.

annotations.label: minimal
annotations.definition: Being smallest in amount, extent or degree.

annotations.label: nodular
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting a knot-like mass.

annotations.label: osseous
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing bone.

annotations.label: scirrhous
annotations.definition: A hardness quality of being rigid and resistant to pressure and fibrous.

annotations.label: serous
annotations.definition: A quality inhering a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing or resembling serum.

annotations.label: neoplastic, invasive
annotations.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition to invade surrounding tissues.

annotations.label: neoplastic, deeply invasive
annotations.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition to invade surrounding tissues to a large extend.

annotations.label: abduction
annotations.definition: A displaced angular placement quality inhering in a body part by virtue of the bearer's movement away from the medial plane of the body.

annotations.label: neoplastic, non-invasive
annotations.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition not to invade surrounding tissues.

annotations.label: adduction
annotations.definition: A displaced angular placement quality inhering in a body part by virtue of the bearer's movement closer the medial plane of the body.
annotations.comment: This term is also used in reference to the operation of the muscle in anatomy or musculature.

annotations.label: fibrillary
annotations.definition: A composition quaity inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's containing of or pertaining to fibrils, or nanoscale fibers.

annotations.label: delayed closure
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's delay to complete the closure of the opening of an anatomical entity.

annotations.label: radiopacity
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's relative inability of electromagnetism to pass through a particular material, particularly X-rays.

annotations.label: radiopaque
annotations.definition: A radiopacity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's prevention of the passage of electromagnetic radiation.

annotations.label: edibility
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to being fit to be eaten.

annotations.label: edible
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being suitable for use as food.

annotations.label: inedible
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer not being suitable for use as food.

annotations.label: irregular density
annotations.definition: A density quality which lacks pattern.

annotations.label: increased radiopacity
annotations.definition: A radiopacity that is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased radiopacity
annotations.definition: A radiopacity that is relatively low.

annotations.label: virulence
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the severity of infectious disease caused by the bearer in a target organism.

annotations.label: reduced virulence
annotations.definition: A virulence that is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased virulence
annotations.definition: A virulence that is relatively high.

annotations.label: ivory
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer exhibiting increased density resulting in exhibiting increased degree of radiopacity (in X-rays).

annotations.label: splayed caudal
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved outwards from the part of the body from which the tail arises.

annotations.label: splayed dorsal
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved outwards from the back or upper surface of an organism.

annotations.label: splayed lateral
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved outwards from the side.

annotations.label: splayed rostral
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved outwards the front of an organism.

annotations.label: splayed ventral
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved outwards the abdomen of an organism.

annotations.label: medially rotated
annotations.definition: A rotation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of being rotated towards the middle of an organism.

annotations.label: laterally rotated
annotations.definition: A rotation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of being rotated towards the side of an organism.

annotations.label: partially dislocated
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being slightly out of its usual or proper place, or position.

annotations.label: bicornuate
annotations.definition: Having two horns or horn-shaped branches.

annotations.label: high-arched
annotations.definition: A concave quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's forming or resembling an arch of an increased apical height.

annotations.label: herniated into
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's bodily structure that has protruded through an abnormal opening in the wall that contains it into the surface or boundary of another entity.

annotations.label: curved medial
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved towards the middle.

annotations.label: drooping
annotations.definition: To bent or hang downwards.

annotations.label: premature closure
annotations.definition: A duration quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's premature completion of the closure of the opening of an anatomical entity.

annotations.label: herniated out of
annotations.definition: A protruding quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's bodily structure that has protruded out of the surface or boundary of another entity.

annotations.label: displaced to
annotations.definition: An angular placement quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being changed in position in relation to another entity.

annotations.label: split medially
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being split along the median axis.

annotations.label: split laterally
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being split asymmetrically on one side.

annotations.label: split bilaterally
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being split on two side.

annotations.label: split radially
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being split along the radial axis.

annotations.label: misaligned towards
annotations.definition: An alignment quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being in a improper spatial positioning towards an additional entity.

annotations.label: misaligned away from
annotations.definition: An alignment quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being in a improper spatial positioning away from an additional entity.

annotations.label: spatial deviation
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's changed direction of position towards another entity.

annotations.label: deviation towards the lateral side
annotations.definition: A deviation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's changed direction of position towards the side.

annotations.label: deviation towards the medial side
annotations.definition: A deviation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's changed direction of position towards the middle.

annotations.label: mislocalised radially
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being out of its usual or proper place, or position around a central point or axis.

annotations.label: mislocalised proximally
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards to point of attachment or origin.

annotations.label: straight
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being free of curves, bends, or angles.

annotations.label: displaced
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue the bearer's being changed in position.

annotations.label: molecular quality
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in a molecular entity, a single molecule, atom, ion, radical etc.

annotations.label: cyclicity
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of whether that there is a presence of cycles in the connection of the atoms within the molecular entity.

annotations.label: cyclic cyclicity
annotations.definition: Cyclic cyclicity inheres in a molecule when the atoms of the molecule do contain at least one cycle in the atom-atom connection paths (through bonds).

annotations.label: acyclic cyclicity
annotations.definition: Acyclic cyclicity inheres in a molecule when the atoms within the molecule do not contain at least one cycle in the atom-atom connection paths (through the bonds).

annotations.label: polarity
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of whether or not the molecular entity has a separation of electric charge which leads to the molecule having an electric dipole.

annotations.label: polar polarity
annotations.definition: Polar polarity is a quality that inheres in a molecular entity when the molecular entity is polar, i.e. does possess an electric dipole.

annotations.label: nonpolar polarity
annotations.definition: A molecular quality which inheres in a molecular entity when the molecular entity does not possess an electrical dipole.

annotations.label: electric polarizability
annotations.definition: The ease of distortion of the electron cloud of a molecular entity by an electric field.

annotations.label: aromaticity
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of the presence of a conjugated ring of unsaturated bonds, lone pairs, or empty orbitals that exhibit a stabilization stronger than would be expected by the stabilization of conjugation alone.

annotations.label: aromatic
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that inheres in a molecular entity when it possesses at least one ring that is aromatic, i.e. A conjugated ring of unsaturated bonds, lone pairs, or empty orbitals that exhibit a stabilization stronger than would be expected by the stabilization of conjugation alone.

annotations.label: non-aromatic
annotations.definition: A quality that inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of the molecule possessing no rings that are aromatic.

annotations.label: electric charge
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of the overall electric charge of the molecule, which is due to a comparison between the total number of electrons and the total number of protons.

annotations.label: neutral charge
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of the molecular entity possessing the same amount of electrons overall as protons, thus having an overall neutral charge.

annotations.label: positive charge
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of the molecular entity possessing more protons overall than electrons, thus having an overall positive charge.

annotations.label: negative charge
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in a molecular entity by virtue of the molecular entity possessing more electrons overall than protons, thus having an overall negative charge.

annotations.label: electronegativity
annotations.definition: A quality that describes the power of an atom to attract electrons to itself .

annotations.label: quality of a substance
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of its constitution.

annotations.label: meltability
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in a substance by virtue of the ease with which the substance can be changed from a solid to a liquid state especially by the application of heat.

annotations.label: vaporizability
annotations.definition: A quality which inheres in a substance by virtue of the ease with which the substance can be changed into a gaseous state.

annotations.label: handedness
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality inhering ina bearer by virtue of the bearer's unequal distribution of fine motor skill between its left and right hands or feet.
annotations.comment: Adapted from Wikipedia and the birnlex term that is dbxref'd. Added on behalf of OBI at March 2010 workshop.

annotations.label: left handedness
annotations.definition: Handedness where the organism preferentially uses the left hand or foot for tasks requiring the use of a single hand or foot or a dominant hand or foot.

annotations.label: right handedness
annotations.definition: Handedness where the organism preferentially uses the right hand or foot for tasks requiring the use of a single hand or foot or a dominant hand or foot.

annotations.label: ambidextrous handedness
annotations.definition: Handedness where the organism exhibits no overall dominance in the use of right or left hand or foot in the performance of tasks that require one hand or foot or a dominant hand or foot.

annotations.label: capacitance
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to hold an electrical charge.
annotations.comment: Capacitance is also a measure of the amount of electrical energy stored (or separated) for a given electric potential.

annotations.label: neonatal
annotations.definition: A maturity quality inhering to a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being at the point or short after birth.

annotations.label: insertion depth
annotations.definition: A quality that is the distance between the end of the inserted object and the surface of the object into which it penetrates.

annotations.label: spoon-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of a spoon.

annotations.label: incisiform
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having the shape of an incisor tooth.

annotations.label: bulbous
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a teardrop.

annotations.label: recurved
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved backward or inward.

annotations.label: slender
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being small or narrow in circumference or width in proportion to length or height.

annotations.label: obclavate
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape being inversely clavate.

annotations.label: bilobed
annotations.definition: A lobed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being divided into or having two lobes.

annotations.label: falciform
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having the shape of a scythe or sickle.

annotations.label: ubiquinated
annotations.definition: A molecular quality that pertains to the post-translational modification of a protein by the covalent attachment of one or more ubiquitin monomer.

annotations.label: nitrated
annotations.definition: A molecular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being combined, or impregnated, with nitric acid, or some of its compounds.

annotations.label: demyelinated
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's loss of myelin sheath.
annotations.comment: For example loss of myelin sheath from a normally myelinated axon or dendrite.

annotations.label: fan-shaped
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer that is shaped in the form of a fan.

annotations.label: phosphorylated
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a protein or a molecule by virtue of the bearer's having a phosphate (PO4) group.

annotations.label: increased phosphorylation
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being phosphorylated to a more than normal extent, or fully saturated with phosphate groups.

annotations.label: decreased phosphorylation
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being phosphorylated to a lesser than normal extent, or less than fully.

annotations.label: oxidized
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having reacted with oxygen, or been modified by oxidation (the reaction in which the atoms of an element lose electrons and the valence of the element increases).

annotations.label: V-shaped
annotations.definition: An angular shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a single angle in its length giving the bearer the form of the letter V.

annotations.label: L-shaped
annotations.definition: An angular shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a single angle in its length giving the bearer the form of the letter L.

annotations.label: subcylindrical
annotations.definition: A cylindrical shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being imperfectly cylindrical or approximately cylindrical.

annotations.label: cup-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape form resembling the shape of a cup.

annotations.label: acuminate
annotations.definition: A tapered shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's tapering gradually to a sharp point.

annotations.label: subrectangular
annotations.definition: A quadrangular shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being approximately rectangular.

annotations.label: subtriangular
annotations.definition: A triangular shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being nearly, but not perfectly, triangular.

annotations.label: multifid
annotations.definition: A split shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or being divided into many lobes or similar segments.

annotations.label: semicircular
annotations.definition: A 2-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having shape or form of half a circle.

annotations.label: far from
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located far (not close to) in space in relation to another entity.

annotations.label: emarginate
annotations.definition: A notched shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a notched tip or edge.

annotations.label: blade-like
annotations.definition: A tapered shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being flat, slender, and tapering to a point.

annotations.label: aliform
annotations.definition: Shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped like a wing.

annotations.label: retrorse
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved backward or downward.

annotations.label: antrorse
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved forward or upward.

annotations.label: hourglass-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape that inheres in the bearer by virtue of the bearer's nearly) symmetric shape wide at its ends and narrow in the middle, resembling the figure of number 8.

annotations.label: teardrop-shaped
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape resembling falling drop.

annotations.label: trilobed
annotations.definition: A lobed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being divided into or having three lobes.

annotations.label: velocity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's rate of change of the position.

annotations.label: fluid flow rate
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a fluid (liquid or gas) by virtue of the amount of fluid which passes through a given surface per unit time.

annotations.label: mass flow rate
annotations.definition: A flow rate quality inhering in a substance by virtue of the mass of substance which passes through a given surface per unit time.

annotations.label: increased force
annotations.definition: A force which relative high.

annotations.label: decreased force
annotations.definition: A force which is relative low.

annotations.label: degree of pigmentation
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having or lacking of substances produced by living organisms that have a color resulting from selective color absorption.

annotations.label: pigmented
annotations.definition: A degree of pigmentation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having substances produced by living organisms that have a color resulting from selective color absorption.

annotations.label: unpigmented
annotations.definition: A degree of pigmentation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking substances produced by living organisms that have a color resulting from selective color absorption.

annotations.label: increased pigmentation
annotations.definition: A degree of pigmentation quality that is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased pigmentation
annotations.definition: A degree of pigmentation quality that is relative low.

annotations.label: antero-posteriorly flattened
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being flattened along the antero-posterior axis.

annotations.label: platelike
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being roundish, flattish shape, possibly with a slightly angled edge.

annotations.label: flattened
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's surface becoming more extended in a plane.
annotations.comment: Becoming flat but not necessarily completely flat.

annotations.label: grooved
annotations.definition: Texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked with one or more channels.

annotations.label: tricuspidate
annotations.definition: A cuscpidate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer possessing three cusps.

annotations.label: multicuspidate
annotations.definition: A cuscpidate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer possessing more than one cusp.

annotations.label: pointed
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a point.

annotations.label: adjacent to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located near in space in relation to another entity.

annotations.label: semi erect
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being partially upright in position or posture.

annotations.label: located in
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's location within another entity.

annotations.label: phosphorylation
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a protein or a molecule by virtue of the addition of a phosphate (PO4) group to the bearer.

annotations.label: dephosphorylated
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a protein or a molecule by virtue of the bearer's lacking a phosphate (PO4) group.

annotations.label: organization quality
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being arranged in a systematic fashion.

annotations.label: behavioural activity
annotations.definition: A behavioral quality of a process inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to exhibit marked activity.

annotations.label: 3-D shape
annotations.definition: A shape that inheres in a 3 dimensional entity.

annotations.label: centered
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located in a position equidistant from edges.

annotations.label: accumulation
annotations.definition: A structural quality of the collection or massing of one physical object within another physical object.

annotations.label: increased accumulation
annotations.definition: An increased number of physical objects that are accumulated within another physical object usually as a result of a failure to break down or remove objects in a timely manner.

annotations.label: decreased accumulation
annotations.definition: An accumulation which is relative low.

annotations.label: parallel to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located at the same continuous distance relative to another object.

annotations.label: marbled
annotations.definition: Multicolored quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being colored with a variegated pattern resembling marble,.

annotations.label: mottled
annotations.definition: Multicolored quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being dappled with spots, patches, or blotches of different colors.

annotations.label: high contrast color pattern
annotations.definition: A color pattern quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's color pattern in which light and dark colors (for example white and black) are codistributed to create a visual impression.

annotations.label: barred
annotations.definition: A color pattern inhering in a bearer by virtue of bearer's exhibiting vertical bars of one hue or degree of saturation crossing another.

annotations.label: iridescent
annotations.definition: A quality of certain surfaces which appear to change colour as the angle of view changes.

annotations.label: articulated with
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's participating in a joint with another entity.

annotations.label: tightly articulated with
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being strongly articulated with another entity.

annotations.label: broadly articulated with
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's possessing a broad surface in articulation with another entity.

annotations.label: biscupidate
annotations.definition: A cuscpidate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer possessing two cusps.

annotations.label: increased mobility
annotations.definition: A mobility which is relative high.

annotations.label: decreased mobility
annotations.definition: A mobility which is relative low.

annotations.label: tension
annotations.definition: A physical quality that pertains by virtue of a pulling force that is directed away from the bearer and attempts to stretch or elongate the bearer.

annotations.label: increased branchiness
annotations.definition: Branchiness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of increasing the degree to which there are subdivisions or offshoots in a bearer entity.

annotations.label: decreased branchiness
annotations.definition: Branchiness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of decreasing the degree to which there are subdivisions or offshoots in a bearer entity.

annotations.label: increased elasticity
annotations.definition: An elasticity which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased elasticity
annotations.definition: An elasticity which is relatively low.

annotations.label: setose
annotations.definition: A hairy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered with setae.
annotations.comment: Setose as a label is used extensively to describe insect phenotypes that have hairy-like appearances (i.e., they are covered in setae, not the hairs of mammals). A bristle is a type of seta.

annotations.label: aplastic/hypoplastic
annotations.definition: Decreased size or absence of organ, tissue or cell due to a reduction in cell numbers.

annotations.label: agenesis
annotations.definition: Absence of a tissue or organ due to failure to develop.

annotations.label: transient
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's underlying structure being capable of change.
annotations.comment: For example, structures undergoing endochondral ossification change in composition from cartilaginous to ossified.

annotations.label: permanent
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's underlying structure not changing over time.

annotations.label: scrobiculate
annotations.definition: A texture that is uniformly covered in short, oblong, or trench-like depressions.

annotations.label: areolate
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape inhering in a surface by virtue of the bearer's being divided by ridge-like structures into a number of small, irregular spaces.

annotations.label: foveate
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a surface by virtue of the bearer's being marked by the presence of small, shallow, regular depressions called fovae.

annotations.label: decreased cellular motility
annotations.definition: A cellular motility which is lower relative to the normal or average.

annotations.label: increased cellular motility
annotations.definition: A cellular motility which is higher relative to the normal or average.

annotations.label: tubular
annotations.definition: A cylindrical shape that is hollow.

annotations.label: increased quality
annotations.definition: A quality that has a value that is increased compared to normal or average.

annotations.label: decreased quality
annotations.definition: A quality that has a value that is decreased compared to normal or average.

annotations.label: decreased process quality
annotations.definition: A quality of a process that has a value that is decreased compared to normal or average.

annotations.label: decreased object quality
annotations.definition: A quality of an object that has a value that is decreased compared to normal or average.

annotations.label: increased process quality
annotations.definition: A quality of a process that has a value that is increased compared to normal or average.

annotations.label: increased object quality
annotations.definition: A quality of an object that has a value that is increased compared to normal or average.

annotations.label: equilateral triangular
annotations.definition: A triangular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having all sides of the same length.

annotations.label: isosceles triangular
annotations.definition: A triangular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two sides have the same length.

annotations.label: scalene triangular
annotations.definition: A triangular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having all sides of unequal length.

annotations.label: fiber shaped
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a consistently sized and approximately round cross-section along its length, which is many times larger than its diameter.
annotations.comment: Note that a fiber shaped object may take any circuitous or straight path through space (think of a length of string or rope).

annotations.label: robust
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being strong, thick or sturdy.

annotations.label: fimbriated
annotations.definition: Having a fringe or border of hairlike or fingerlike projections.

annotations.label: segmented
annotations.definition: Consisting of segments or similar parts arranged in a longitudinal series.

annotations.label: telescopic
annotations.definition: Having or resembling the shape of a telescope.

annotations.label: ligamentous
annotations.definition: Having the nature or characteristic of, a ligament; composed of the tissue proper to ligaments.

annotations.label: brush-like shape
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling the end of a brush with a multitude of bristles.

annotations.label: autogenous
annotations.definition: Being separate or discrete from other skeletal elements.

annotations.label: parallelogram
annotations.definition:
A convex quadrilateral is a parallelogram if and only if any one of the following statements are true:
1. Each diagonal divides the quadrilateral into two congruent triangles with the same orientation.
2. The opposite sides are congruent in pairs.
3. The diagonals bisect each other.
4. The opposite angles are congruent in pairs.
5. The sum of the squares of the sides equals the sum of the squares of the diagonals. (This is the parallelogram law)
6.It possesses rotational symmetry.
7.One pair of opposite sides are parallel and congruent.
8.Two pairs of adjacent angles are supplementary.

annotations.label: superelliptic
annotations.definition: A shape constituting a transition between a rectangle and a circle; a closed curve, of which the circle and ellipse are special cases, whose parametric equation is x = a.cos2/rt, y = b.cos2/rt

annotations.label: squircle
annotations.definition: A shape with properties between those of a square and those of a circle.

annotations.label: diamond shaped
annotations.definition: A superelliptic shape inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape resembling a diamond.

annotations.label: temporal distribution quality
annotations.definition: A temporal distribution pattern of process occurrences within a regulation/reference process.

annotations.label: offset quality
annotations.definition: The temporal relation between the end of the process with respect to a reference process.

annotations.label: onset quality
annotations.definition: The temporal relation between the start of the process with respect to a reference process.

annotations.label: angle
annotations.definition: An orientation inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's placement at an angle.

annotations.label: increased angle to
annotations.definition: An angle which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased angle to
annotations.definition: An angle which is relatively low.

annotations.label: hyponastic
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a upward bending of its leaves or other plant parts.

annotations.label: oblanceolate
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being extended along a straight line, and is shaped like a reversed lance-point, with the tapering point attached to the leafstalk.

annotations.label: adhesive
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer exhibiting molecular attraction to another entity in contact.

annotations.label: non-adhesive
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer not exhibiting molecular attraction to another entity in contact.

annotations.label: increased adhesivity
annotations.definition: A adhesivity which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased adhesivity
annotations.definition: A adhesivity which is relatively low.

annotations.label: tholiform
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being dome-shaped.

annotations.label: pyramidal
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having triangular faces that meet at a common point and containing a polygonal shaped base.

annotations.label: lance-ovate
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a continuous convex surface with an axis of symmetry and one axis longer than the other; characterized with an egg-shaped form crossed with a lance-head shaped form.

annotations.label: lanceolate-triangular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped like a lance-head, considerably longer than wide, tapering towards the tip from below the middle together forming three angles; attached at the broad end.

annotations.label: hispid
annotations.definition: A quality of being covered with stiff or rough hairs.

annotations.label: hispidulous
annotations.definition: A quality of being minutely hispid.

annotations.label: tomentose
annotations.definition: A quality of being covered with short, dense, matted hairs.

annotations.label: cupulate
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being a cupule, with a structure similar to a cup that also encloses the ovule in partiality.

annotations.label: decumbent
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lying or growing on the ground but with erect or rising tips.

annotations.label: arachnose
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being characterized by fine, entangled hairs giving the appearance of cobweb.

annotations.label: lemon-shaped
annotations.definition: A convex 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a quadric surface in three dimensions obtained by rotating less than half of a circular arc about an axis passing through the endpoints of the arc

annotations.label: snowman-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two connected parts, roughly spherical, of different sizes.

annotations.label: obconical
annotations.definition: A conical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a cone, with the pointed end attached to an organism.

annotations.label: ligneous
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer resembling wood.

annotations.label: basal to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the base of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: cauline to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the stalk of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: fleshy
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer resembling or containing flesh.

annotations.label: herbaceous
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's upper parts die back to the rootstock at the end of the growing season.

annotations.label: activation quality
annotations.definition: A quality that inheres in a bearer in virtue of its realizing one of its functions.

annotations.label: active
annotations.definition: A quality of an physical object that is currently realizing one of its functions.

annotations.label: inactive
annotations.definition: A quality of a physical object that is currently realizing none of its functions.

annotations.label: constitutively active
annotations.definition: A quality of a physical object that has been constantly realizing some of its functions.

annotations.label: D-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a strait side and an arched side giving the bearer the form of the letter D.

annotations.label: ridged
annotations.definition: An elongated raised margin or border.

annotations.label: broad
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's width being notably higher than its length.

annotations.label: tendency
annotations.definition: A disposition that can be compared on a linear scale - that is that it can be increased or decreased.

annotations.label: increased tendency
annotations.definition: A tendency which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased tendency
annotations.definition: A tendency which is relatively low.

annotations.label: bronze
annotations.definition: A medium brown color with a metallic sheen, resembles the actual alloy bronze.

annotations.label: shortened
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's length being notably lower than its width.

annotations.label: intact female
annotations.definition: Female quality of having complete, unaltered reproductive organs

annotations.label: intact male
annotations.definition: Male quality of having complete, unaltered reproductive organs.

annotations.label: castrated male
annotations.definition: Male quality of being incapable to reproduce because of functional loss of the testicles from surgical removal or chemical means.

annotations.label: nulliparous
annotations.definition: A viviparous organism having never given birth.

annotations.label: multiparous
annotations.definition: A viviparous organism that has given birth two or more times.

annotations.label: parity
annotations.definition: The number of time that a viviparous organism has given birth.

annotations.label: primiparous
annotations.definition: A viviparous organism having given birth one time.

annotations.label: grand multiparous
annotations.definition: A viviparous organism that has given birth five or more times.

annotations.label: mediolateral polarity
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's location of features or characteristics along the mediolateral axis.

annotations.label: fluctuating
annotations.definition: Recurrent and often more or less cyclic alteration.

annotations.label: trumpet-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape form resembling the shape of a trumpet.

annotations.label: inflated
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being filled with air.

annotations.label: uninflated
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being not filed with air.

annotations.label: acinus
annotations.definition: Having a shape that resembles a many-lobed "berry," such as a raspberry.

annotations.label: ovariohysterectomized female
annotations.definition: A female fertility quality inhering in a female by virtue of the bearer's not being capable of reproducing because of surgical removal of ovaries and uterus.

annotations.label: ovariectomized female
annotations.definition: A female fertility quality inhering in a female by virtue of the bearer's not being capable of reproducing because of surgical removal of ovaries.

annotations.label: waxiness
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the presence of long chain aliphatic lipids on the surface of an organism.

annotations.label: increased waxiness
annotations.definition: A waxiness which is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased waxiness
annotations.definition: A waxiness which is relatively low.

annotations.label: waxy
annotations.definition: Presence of long chain aliphatic lipids on the surface of an organism.

annotations.label: striate-angular
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by narrow lines, usually parallel, with at least one salient angle on the margin

annotations.label: anvil
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape that inheres in the bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape that is wider at one end and narrow in the middle.

annotations.label: peracute
annotations.definition: Extremely acute and aggressive onset of a pathological process.

annotations.label: pedunculate
annotations.definition: Being supported by a peduncle or stalk.

annotations.label: procumbent
annotations.definition: Growing horizontal to the ground without setting roots.

annotations.label: radius
annotations.definition: A length quality which is equal to the length of any straight line segment that passes from the center of a circle to any endpoint on the circular boundary. The radius is half of the diameter.

annotations.label: triradiate
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having three rays or radiating branches.

annotations.label: diagonal to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located on a sloping line joining two nonconsecutive points of an entity relative to another entity.

annotations.label: increased tolerance to
annotations.definition: A tolerance quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's increased ability to endure a stimulus.

annotations.label: decreased tolerance to
annotations.definition: A tolerance quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's decreased ability to endure a stimulus.

annotations.label: mislocalized abaxially
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned away from the axis of an organism.

annotations.label: mislocalized adaxially
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned towards the axis of an organism.

annotations.label: subcircular
annotations.definition: A circular shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being nearly, but not perfectly, circular.

annotations.label: anteromedially rotated
annotations.definition: A rotation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of being rotated towards the front and the middle of an organism.

annotations.label: fusiform
annotations.definition: Having a spindle-like shape that is wide in the middle and tapers at both ends.

annotations.label: random pattern
annotations.definition: characterised by an unidentifiable pattern.

annotations.label: multifocal to coalescing
annotations.definition: Multiple lesions some interconnecting with each other.

annotations.label: generalized
annotations.definition: Affecting all regions without specificity of distribution.

annotations.label: segmental
annotations.definition: Affecting a segment or segments.

annotations.label: friability
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to be reduced to smaller pieces with little effort.

annotations.label: friable
annotations.definition: Easily reduced to powder or easily fragmentable or crumbly.

annotations.label: indurated
annotations.definition: Having become firm or hard especially by increase of fibrous elements.

annotations.label: watery
annotations.definition: Having the consistency of water.

annotations.label: fluid-filled
annotations.definition: Bounded structure filled with fluid.

annotations.label: beige
annotations.definition: A colour that is very pale yellowish brown.

annotations.label: yellow brown
annotations.definition: A color consisting of yellow and brown hue.

annotations.label: dark yellow brown
annotations.definition: A color consisting of yellow and brown hue and low brightness.

annotations.label: light yellow brown
annotations.definition: A color consisting of yellow and brown hue and high brightness.

annotations.label: chronic-active
annotations.definition: A quality of duration having progressive course of indefinite duration with active, acute-like elements.

annotations.label: focally extensive
annotations.definition: Single lesion with expansion into surrounding tissue.

annotations.label: sunken
annotations.definition: Flattened from above downward; below the normal level or the level of the surrounding parts.

annotations.label: transmural
annotations.definition: Extending through or affecting the entire thickness of the wall of an organ or cavity or boundary.

annotations.label: acidophilic
annotations.definition: An affinity inhering in an anatomical structure by virtue of the bearer exhibiting a molecular interaction for acidic dyes under specific pH conditions.
annotations.comment: Eosin stains acidophilic structures, hence eosinophilic is the same as acidophilic.

annotations.label: amphiphilic
annotations.definition: Having both hydrophilic and hydrophobic (or lipophilic) groups.

annotations.label: cicatricial
annotations.definition: Having or resembling the characteristic of a cicatrix - a scar resulting from formation and contraction of fibrous tissue.

annotations.label: acinar
annotations.definition: Pertaining to the individual parts making up an aggregate fruit like a many-lobed "berry," such as a raspberry.
annotations.comment: Acinus is Latin for berry.

annotations.label: papillomatous
annotations.definition: A round or lobulated protruding form resembling a nipple.

annotations.label: divergent from
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two or more entities that extend from a common point in different directions.

annotations.label: exposed
annotations.definition: Open to view or not covered by another entity.

annotations.label: scaphoid
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped like a boat.

annotations.label: knobbled
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape having numerous very small circular rounded projections or protuberances.

annotations.label: tripodal
annotations.definition: A 3-D shape with three supports or legs.

annotations.label: x-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of the letter X.

annotations.label: strap-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality in which the bearer is shaped like a narrow band; ligulate.

annotations.label: waisted
annotations.definition: A shape quality in which the bearer possesses a gradual, yet distinct, thinning from a wide starting point.

annotations.label: spade-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape that is in the form of a spade.

annotations.label: sculpted surface
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape marked by large amounts of relief, often with multiple ridges and grooves in close association. Topographically complex.

annotations.label: perpendicular to
annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located at a right angle relative to another entity.

annotations.label: abutting
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer touching another entity along a border or with a projecting part.

annotations.label: offset
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's position being displaced from a reference point.

annotations.label: interlocked with
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being locked together or interconnected to another entity.

annotations.label: subdermal
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located or placed beneath the skin.

annotations.label: w-shaped
annotations.definition: An angular shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having two angles in its length giving the bearer the form of the letter W.

annotations.label: ornamentation
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the degree of the bearer's highly topographical with ridges, pits, rugosity or other surface structures.

annotations.label: ornamented
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape in which the bearer's surface is highly topographical with ridges, pits, rugosity or other surface structures.

annotations.label: unornamented
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape in which the bearer's surface is lacking ridges, pits, rugosity or other surface structures.

annotations.label: level with
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located at the same level as another entity.

annotations.label: mineralized
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being composed of or possessing inorganic material.

annotations.label: torsioned
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being twisted or turned.
annotations.comment: The word "torsion" has multiple different meanings in different context (e.g. the act of twisting or the morphology of the digestive tract in gastropods). This term refers purely to the resulting shape and is not to be used in the engineering and mechanics sense of torsion. \nNote: kinked PATO:0001798(synonym twisted) is similar to 'torsioned', but it is different in that something that is kinked has sharp twists or turns.

annotations.label: anchor-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a shape resembling an anchor.

annotations.label: quadripartite
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being divided into four parts.

annotations.label: oriented towards
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being aligned or positioned towards another entity.

annotations.label: posteromedial to
