annotations.definition: A spatial quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located toward the rear and mid-line surface of an organism relative to another entity.

annotations.label: firm
annotations.definition: A hardness quality of giving significant resistance to pressure.

annotations.label: sexually dimorphic
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a phenotypic difference between males and females of the same species.

annotations.label: decondensed
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being thinner or more loosely packed.

annotations.label: eroding
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer bring gradually worn away.

annotations.label: carpel-like
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's similarity to the appearance of a carpel.

annotations.label: corymb-like
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's architecture bearing resemblence to a corymb type of inflorescence.

annotations.label: inflorescence-like
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's similarity to the appearance of an inflorescence.

annotations.label: leaf-like
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's similarity to the appearance of a leaf.

annotations.label: pin-like
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being thin and lacking outgrowths.

annotations.label: sepal-like
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's similarity to the appearance of a sepal,

annotations.label: shriveled
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being smaller and contracted, especially due to loss of moisture.

annotations.label: wilty
annotations.definition: To bend or hang downwards due to loss of water

annotations.label: collagenous
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting of collagen.

annotations.label: extends to
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of some aspect of the bearer extending to the position of another entity.

annotations.label: extends beyond
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of some aspect of the bearer extending past the position of another entity.

annotations.label: prism shaped
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling a prism.

annotations.label: keyhole shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer resembling a keyhole.

annotations.label: lathlike
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer resembling a thin narrow strip.

annotations.label: distensible
annotations.definition: A structure quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being capable of swelling or stretching.

annotations.label: sutured to
annotations.definition: Structural quality inhering in a bearer in which a joint has apposed bony surfaces united by fibrous tissue, permitting no movement; found only between bones.

annotations.label: transformed to
annotations.definition: A morphological quality between two bearers inhering that one bearer is subject to change the nature, function, or condition of itself to become the other.

annotations.label: increased velocity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's increased rate of change of the position.

annotations.label: decreased velocity
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's decreased rate of change of the position.

annotations.label: neoplastic, spontaneous
annotations.definition: A disposition inhering in a tumour by virtue of the bearer's disposition to spontaneously arise.

annotations.label: bitter
annotations.definition: A flavor quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an acrid, sharp, or disagreeable flavor.

annotations.label: subterminal
annotations.definition: A position quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's existing near the end of another entity.

annotations.label: terminal
annotations.definition: A position quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's forming the end of another entity.

annotations.label: brittle
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to breaks without significant deformation (strain) when subjected to stress.

annotations.label: transversely striated
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by narrow lines or grooves, usually parallel, that are oriented transversely relative to the long axis of the bearer.

annotations.label: obliquely striated
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by narrow lines or grooves, usually parallel, that are oriented obliquely relative to the long axis of the bearer.

annotations.label: poorly ossified
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in an bearer by virtue of the bearer's possessing only minor levels of ossification.

annotations.label: oblique orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a slanting direction or position : neither perpendicular nor parallel.

annotations.label: slit-like
annotations.definition: Shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer exhibiting a narrow and elongate opening.

annotations.label: external to
annotations.definition: Positional quality in which an entity is located outside of another entity.

annotations.label: inverted-V shaped
annotations.definition: An angular shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a single angle in its length giving the bearer the form of an inverted letter V.

annotations.label: parallel-sided
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's possessing two sides that are at the same continuous distance relative to each other.

annotations.label: multi-organismal process quality
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a process by virtue of that process having two or more organisms as participants.

annotations.label: single organismal process quality
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a process by virtue of that process having exactly one organism as a participant.

annotations.label: overlapping
annotations.definition: Overlap with quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned over another entity so as to cover some portion of it.

annotations.label: overlapped by
annotations.definition: Overlap with quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being positioned under another entity so as to be covered by some portion of it.

annotations.label: anterior orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed anteriorly.

annotations.label: anterodorsal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed anterodorsally.

annotations.label: anterolateral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed anterolaterally.

annotations.label: anteroventral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed anteroventrally.

annotations.label: distal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed distally.

annotations.label: dorsal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed dorsally.

annotations.label: dorsolateral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed dorsolaterally.

annotations.label: lateral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed laterally.

annotations.label: medial orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed medially.

annotations.label: posterior orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed posteriorly.

annotations.label: ventrolaterally orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed ventrolaterally.

annotations.label: ventral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed ventrally.

annotations.label: transverse orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed transversely.

annotations.label: posterolateral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed posterolaterally.

annotations.label: posterodorsal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed posterodorsally.

annotations.label: nucleated
annotations.definition: A nucleate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having one or more nucleus.

annotations.label: alobate
annotations.definition: A surface feature shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's not having undulating edges forming lobes.

annotations.label: keratinous
annotations.definition: A composition quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's consisting of keratin.

annotations.label: concealed
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being hidden from view.

annotations.label: hexagonal
annotations.definition: A 2-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having six angles and six sides.

annotations.label: multipartite
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being divided into multiple parts.

annotations.label: cane-like
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's similarity to the appearance of a cane.

annotations.label: confluent with
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer flowing or coming together with another entity.

annotations.label: oblique to
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a slanting direction (neither perpendicular nor parallel) relative to another entity.

annotations.label: anteromedial orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed anteromedially.

annotations.label: increased elevation relative to
annotations.definition: An elevation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue the bearer's being in a high position relative to another entity.

annotations.label: concealed by
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being hidden from view or covered by another entity.

annotations.label: saddle-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's similarity to the appearance of a saddle.

annotations.label: flush
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being level or even with another surface.

annotations.label: cancellous
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an open, latticed, or porous structure; used especially of bone.

annotations.label: scute-like
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being shaped like a scute or an osteoderm.

annotations.label: funnel-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's similarity to the appearance of a funnel.

annotations.label: keel-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having an elongate structure, similar to a ridge, that extends longitudinally along an anatomical entity.

annotations.label: vertical to
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being located at a right angle relative to another entity and to the horizon.

annotations.label: mesiolateral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed mesiolaterally.

annotations.label: separating
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being in a position that produces a separation in, or acting to separate, another entity.

annotations.label: anterodistal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed anterodistally.

annotations.label: posterodistal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed posterodistally.

annotations.label: bipartite
annotations.definition: A multipartite quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being divided into two parts.

annotations.label: plowshare shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped like a plowshare.

annotations.label: peg-like
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a short and stout cylindrical shape.

annotations.label: boomerang shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a shape like a boomerang, with two arms connected at an angle.

annotations.label: subovoid
annotations.definition: A spheroid quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting a shape not quite ovoid. approximately ovoid.

annotations.label: concavo-convex
annotations.definition: A shape quality that obtains by virtue of the bearer being concave on one side and convex on the other.

annotations.label: ring shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a shape like a ring (a circular shape enclosing a space).

annotations.label: spur shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's possessing a small, spike-like shape.

annotations.label: homeostatic
annotations.definition: Pertaining to the ability or tendency of an organism or cell to maintain internal equilibrium by adjusting its physiological processes.

annotations.label: borderline intensity
annotations.definition: An intensity which is borderline in it its effect or force as compared to baseline or normal.

annotations.label: profound intensity
annotations.definition: An intensity which is characterized by being very severe.

annotations.label: episodic
annotations.definition: An occurrence quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a course that occurs occasionally.

annotations.label: refractory
annotations.definition: An occurrence of a process during which time there is an inability to change the course, extent, or repetition of the process.

annotations.label: secondary
annotations.definition: A quality in which the effect or outcome is not primary or the focus of the observation.

annotations.label: hemispheroid
annotations.definition: A 3-D shape that is half of a sphere.

annotations.label: variability of count
annotations.definition: A variability quality inhering in the bearer by virtue of the bearer having a variable number or count of an entity.

annotations.label: pear shaped
annotations.definition: Having the shape of a pear; tapering near the top and bulging toward the base or bottom. From thefreedictionary.com

annotations.label: paddle shaped
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's resembling the form of a paddle.

annotations.label: subelliptical
annotations.definition: A shape that is almost an ellipse.

annotations.label: radiating
annotations.definition: Shape quality inhering in a bearer expanding outward, or having parts expanding outward, from a center point.

annotations.label: differentiated from
annotations.definition: A morphology quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being morphologically distinguishable from another entity.

annotations.label: hypsodont
annotations.definition: A 3-D shape inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having dentition characterized by high-crowned teeth and enamel which extends far past the gum line, which provides extra material for wear and tear.
annotations.comment: Need to include axiom: hypsodont inheres_in some ('tooth crown' and bearer_of 'increased \nheight').

annotations.label: brachydont
annotations.definition: A 3-D shape inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having dentition characterized by low-crowned teeth. Human teeth are brachydont.
annotations.comment: Need to include axiom: brachydont inheres_in some ('tooth crown' and bearer_of 'decreased \nheight').

annotations.label: molariform
annotations.definition: A 3-D shape inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an entity in the form of a molar tooth.

annotations.label: plumose
annotations.definition: A branched quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having many fine filaments or branches that give a feathery appearance: for example, resembling a feather.

annotations.label: continuous with
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer possessing an uninterrupted or unbroken connection or spatial distribution relative to the position of another entity.

annotations.label: complete structure
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being fully formed compared to entities lacking parts.

annotations.label: incomplete structure
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's lacking parts or having a reduced form compared to fully formed entities.

annotations.label: subspherical
annotations.definition: A spheroid shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being nearly, but not perfectly, spherical.

annotations.label: tapered size
annotations.definition: A size quality inhering in an entity or set of entities in which size increases or decreases along the extent of the entity or set.

annotations.label: surrounded by
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being enclosed on all sides by another entity.

annotations.label: labiolingually compressed
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being compressed from the side facing the lips to the side facing the tongue.

annotations.label: mesiodistally compressed
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being compressed from the side facing the midline of the jaw to the side opposite the midline.

annotations.label: curved lingually
annotations.definition: A curvature quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being curved towards the tongue.

annotations.label: irregularly shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering on a bearer by virtue of a bearer's shape lacking in pattern or uniformity; for example, it may have sides of different lengths, different curvatures, or unequal angles.

annotations.label: unicuspidate
annotations.definition: Cuspidate quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's possessing a single cusp.

annotations.label: uniform diameter
annotations.definition: A diameter that remains constant throughout the extent of an entity.

annotations.label: parasagittal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is in the parasagittal plane.

annotations.label: longitudinal orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed along the longitudinal or anterior-posterior axis.

annotations.label: maximally connected
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in the bearer by virtue of the bearer consisting of a single, maximally connected structure.
annotations.comment: What counts as maximally connected may be relative to some specification of granularity.

annotations.label: disconnected
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in the bearer by virtue of the bearer consisting of multiple structures lacking any physical connection to each other.

annotations.label: propagation velocity
annotations.definition: The velocity of spatial propagation of a process. Examples include the velocity of propagation of an action potential along an axon.

annotations.label: increased propagation velocity
annotations.definition: A propagation velocity that is higher than normal.

annotations.label: decreased propagation velocity
annotations.definition: A propagation velocity that is lower than normal.

annotations.label: spatial distribution of a process
annotations.definition: A physical quality of a process that is its spatial distribution. For example, bone mineralization follows stereotypical spatial distribution patterns during development, which may be altered in some phenotypes.

annotations.label: cell morphology
annotations.definition: A quality of a single cell inhering in the bearer by virtue of the bearer's size or shape or structure.
annotations.comment: Use this term for morphologies that can *only* inhere in a cell, e.g. morphological qualities inhering in a cell by virtue of the presence, location or shape of one or more cell parts.

annotations.label: multiciliated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having multiple cilia.

annotations.label: monociliated
annotations.definition: A cellular quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having only one cilium.

annotations.label: absence due to degeneration
annotations.definition: Absence due to a degenerative process.

annotations.label: process efficacy
annotations.definition: The ability of a process to produce its output.

annotations.label: decreased efficacy
annotations.definition: A decrease in the ability of a process to produce its output.

annotations.label: increased efficacy
annotations.definition: A increase in the ability of a process to produce its output.

annotations.label: tendrilous
annotations.definition: Slender and spirally coiled.

annotations.label: polymeric
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a structure by virtue of it consisting of many of repeated, similar or identical subunits, arranged in some orderly array.
annotations.comment: A complete coverage of this domain would subclasses homopolymeric, copolymeric, crystalline.

annotations.label: prominence
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the degree to which the bearers stands out or projects beyond a surface or line.

annotations.label: distensibility
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the extent to which the bearer is capable of being stretched or swollen.

annotations.label: humidity
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in air by virtue of the partial pressure exerted by the bearer's water vapour content.

annotations.label: increased humidity
annotations.definition: An increase in humidity.

annotations.label: decreased humidity
annotations.definition: An decrease in humidity.

annotations.label: quality of a plasma
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's exhibiting the physical characteristics of an entity consisting of roughly equal proportions of positively and negatively charged particles, having neither a defined volume nor defined shape, having internal magnetic fields, and being electrically conductive.

annotations.label: degree of illumination
annotations.definition: The degree to which the bearer is exposed to visible light.

annotations.label: increased degree of illumination
annotations.definition: An increase in illumination.

annotations.label: decreased degree of illumination
annotations.definition: An decrease in illumination.

annotations.label: stepped
annotations.definition: Having a shape or form that resembles a step of a stairway (e.g., a ledge or offset margin).

annotations.label: quality of a colloid
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in an entity by virtue of a portion of the bearer's molecular or polymolecular constituents present in one phase being dispersed in a portion of the bearer's constituents present in another phase.

annotations.label: quality of an aerosol
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in an entity by virtue of the bearer's solid or liquid constituents being suspended in a gas.

annotations.label: C-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of the letter C.

annotations.label: J-shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of the letter J.

annotations.label: combustibility
annotations.definition: The propensity of a material to undergo combustion. Combustion encompasses smouldering and flaming combustion. Combustibility is usually applied to solids.

annotations.label: increased combustibility
annotations.definition: An increase in combustibility.

annotations.label: decreased combustibility
annotations.definition: An decrease in combustibility.

annotations.label: increased porosity
annotations.definition: An increase in porosity.

annotations.label: decreased porosity
annotations.definition: An decrease in porosity.

annotations.label: stability
annotations.definition: quality, state, or degree of being stable.

annotations.label: increased stability
annotations.definition: An increase in stability.

annotations.label: decreased stability
annotations.definition: A decrease in stability.

annotations.label: quality of a suspension
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in an entity by virtue of the bearer's solid or liquid constituents being transiently dispersed in a solid, liquid, or gas before settling due to gravity or centrifugal forces.

annotations.label: plume-shaped
annotations.definition: A shape that ineres in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's mass being distributed in a feather-like fashion.

annotations.label: contamination
annotations.definition: The presence of a constituent, impurity, or some other undesirable element that spoils, corrupts, infects, makes unfit, or makes inferior a material, physical body, natural environment, place of human occupancy, or other material entity.

annotations.label: increased contamination
annotations.definition: An increase in contamination.

annotations.label: decreased contamination
annotations.definition: A decrease in contamination.

annotations.label: genotypic sex
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual based upon genotypic composition of sex chromosomes.
annotations.comment: Note that the chromosomal constitution of genotypic sex varies in different organisms. In human, we have XY, XX, though abnormal XXY, X0, XYY, and other combinations are possible.

annotations.label: male genotypic sex
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual based upon genotypic composition that confers the capability for an organism to differentiate male gonads.
annotations.comment: Note that the chromosomal constitution of genotypic sex varies in different organisms. In human, this would be XY, though abnormal XXY and XYY would also result in a male phenotypic outcome.

annotations.label: female genotypic sex
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population based upon genotypic composition that confers the capability for an organism to differentiate female gonads.
annotations.comment: Note that the chromosomal constitution of genotypic sex varies in different organisms. In human, this would be XX, though abnormal X0 and XXX would also result in a female phenotypic outcome.

annotations.label: potability
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to being fit to be used as a safe means of hydration.

annotations.label: non-potable
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer not being suitable for use as a safe means of hydration.

annotations.label: potable
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being suitable for use as a safe means of hydration.

annotations.label: anteriorized
annotations.definition: A malformed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally anterior structures and missing some or all of what are normally posterior structures.

annotations.label: dorsalized
annotations.definition: A malformed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally dorsal structures and missing some or all of what are normally ventral structures.

annotations.label: posteriorized
annotations.definition: A malformed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally posterior structures and missing some or all of what are normally anterior structures.

annotations.label: ventralized
annotations.definition: A malformed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally ventral structures and missing some or all of what are normally dorsal structures.

annotations.label: partially anteriorized
annotations.definition: An anteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally anterior structures and missing some but not all of what are normally posterior structures.

annotations.label: partially dorsalized
annotations.definition: A dorsalized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally dorsal structures and missing some but not all of what are normally ventral structures.

annotations.label: partially posteriorized
annotations.definition: A posteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally posterior structures and missing some but not all of what are normally anterior structures.

annotations.label: partially ventralized
annotations.definition: A ventralized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally ventral structures and missing some but not all of what are normally dorsal structures.

annotations.label: dorso-anteriorized
annotations.definition: A malformed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally dorso-anterior structures and missing some or all of what are normally ventro-posterior structures.

annotations.label: partially dorso-anteriorized
annotations.definition: A dorso-anteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally dorso-anterior structures and missing some but not all of what are normally ventro-posterior structures.

annotations.label: wholly dorso-anteriorized
annotations.definition: A dorso-anteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing only what are normally dorso-anterior structures.

annotations.label: ventro-posteriorized
annotations.definition: A malformed quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally ventro-posterior structures and missing some or all of what are normally dorso-anterior structures.

annotations.label: partially ventro-posteriorized
annotations.definition: A ventro-posteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing what are normally ventro-posterior structures and missing some but not all of what are normally dorso-anterior structures.

annotations.label: wholly ventro-posteriorized
annotations.definition: A ventro-posteriorized quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's gross morphology containing only what are normally ventro-posterior structures.

annotations.label: heterotaxic
annotations.definition: An oriented quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being abnormally placed or arranged.

annotations.label: position originates from
annotations.definition: A positional quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of some aspect of that bearer beginning from a position on another entity.
annotations.comment: Original label "originates from" changed to current label to differentiate that this term is a positional quality rather than one that connects between an entity and another entity it originates from.

annotations.label: pathogenicity
annotations.definition: The ability of a pathogen to produce an infectious disease or disorder in an another organism.

annotations.label: plush
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered or partially covered with hair that has increased thickness and softness.

annotations.label: posteroventral orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed posteroventrally.

annotations.label: crown like
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a shape like a crown.

annotations.label: shell shaped
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having a shape like a shell.

annotations.label: fringed
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having an ornamental border consisting of short straight or twisted threads or strips hanging from cut or raveled edges or from a separate band.

annotations.label: bowl shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being shaped in the form of a bowl.

annotations.label: mobile relative to
annotations.definition: Mobility relative to a second entity.
annotations.comment: For example, distinct anteroposterior mobility of palatine relative to mesethmoid.

annotations.label: immobile relative to
annotations.definition: Immobility relative to a second entity.
annotations.comment: For example: distinct anteroposterior immobility of palatine relative to mesethmoid.

annotations.label: plug shaped
annotations.definition: A shape that is in the form of a plug, being tube-like and expanded on one end.

annotations.label: dorsomedial orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed dorsomedially.

annotations.label: band shaped
annotations.definition: A shape that is in the form of a narrow strip encircling an object.

annotations.label: trough shaped
annotations.definition: A concave 3-D shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's shape resembling that of a trough, being narrow, long, and boxlike in shape.

annotations.label: pentagonal
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having five angles and five sides.

annotations.label: dysplastic growth
annotations.definition: A growth quality of occurrent in which the growth of an organism, structure or group of organisms is abnormal.

annotations.label: fibrotic
annotations.definition: Fibrosis is the formation of excess fibrous connective tissue in an organ or tissue in a reparative or reactive process.

annotations.label: spayed female
annotations.definition: Female quality of being incapable to reproduce because of functional loss of the ovaries and/or uterus from surgical removal.

annotations.label: unpaired
annotations.definition: The bearer of this quality has_part n=1 of the indicated entity type, where n is bilaterally paired for a comparable organism..

annotations.label: flared
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer being expanded laterally and or opened outward in shape.

annotations.label: distomedial orientation
annotations.definition: An orientation quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer having a position that is directed distomedially, ie, pertaining to an inside aspect, but also farther away from the center of the body.

annotations.label: bilaterally paired
annotations.definition: The bearer of this quality has_part n=2 of the indicated entity type, where n is unpaired for a comparable organism.

annotations.label: lesioned
annotations.definition: A structural quality which is held by a bearer where a localized pathological or traumatic structural change, damage, deformity, or discontinuity of tissue, organ, or body part is present.

annotations.label: structurally discontinuous
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being marked by interruptions or gaps.
annotations.comment: This term is intended for use for terms such as MP:0010461 discontinuous pulmonary artery and MP:0004157 interrupted aortic arch.

annotations.label: doubled haploid
annotations.definition: A ploidy quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's haploid cells undergo chromosome doubling.

annotations.label: parous
annotations.definition: An organismal quality of having successfully reproduced.

annotations.label: shaded
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of that bearer being shielded from visible light.
annotations.comment: Shaded does not refer to radiation more generally, as this would be more shielded than shaded.

annotations.label: decreased width and length
annotations.definition: A width and length quality which is relatively small, where both width and length are decreased.

annotations.label: increased width and length
annotations.definition: A width and length quality that is relatively large, where both width and length are increased.

annotations.label: increased linear velocity
annotations.definition: A distance traveled in a specific time that is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased linear velocity
annotations.definition: A distance traveled in a specific time that is relatively low.

annotations.label: fractured
annotations.definition: A broken quality where an entity is cracked or broken, usually a bone.

annotations.label: transversely fractured
annotations.definition: A fractured quality where the fracture traverses the bone perpendicular to the axis of the bone.

annotations.label: impact-fractured
annotations.definition: A fractured quality in which one of the fragments is driven into another fragment.

annotations.label: linearly fractured
annotations.definition: A fractured quality where the fracture runs parallel to the long axis of a bone.

annotations.label: obliquely fractured
annotations.definition: A fractured quality where the fracture occurs at a plane oblique to the long axis of the bone.

annotations.label: spirally fractured
annotations.definition: A fractured quality that occurs when torque (a rotating force) is applied along the axis of a bone.

annotations.label: compression-fractured
annotations.definition: A fractured quality that is caused by a loss of bone mass (osteoporosis) that occurs as part of aging.

annotations.label: avulsion fractured
annotations.definition: A fractured quality in which the fragment of bone tears away from the main mass of bone as a result of physical trauma.

annotations.label: decreased ratio
annotations.definition: A decreased quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's decreased magnitude in proportion to the magnitude of another entity.
annotations.comment: Example: decreased proportion of CD4 T cells relative to total T cells.

annotations.label: increased ratio
annotations.definition: An increased quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's increased magnitude in proportion to the magnitude of another entity.
annotations.comment: Example: increased proportion of CD4 T cells relative to total T cells.

annotations.label: ruptured
annotations.definition: A structural quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's components being torn, typically used for soft tissues or membranes.

annotations.label: population growth rate
annotations.definition: The rate or speed of the change in in number of a population.

annotations.label: single organismal growth rate
annotations.definition: The rate or speed of the change in size of an individual entity.

annotations.label: fragmented
annotations.definition: Existing or functioning as though broken into separate parts; disorganized; disunified.
annotations.comment: Moved to a child of wholeness because broken implies a trauma and fragmented could be caused by a developmental process. See HP:0100168 Fragmented epiphyses as an example.

annotations.label: erythematous
annotations.definition: Red due to increase blood circulation in a defined area.

annotations.label: male with DSD
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population who are otherwise male of a non-hermaphroditic species who has ambiguous or atypical congenital development of the reproductive system.
annotations.comment: The term intersex is popular in use, but may be considered offensive when applied to humans. DSD can be expanded multiple ways to 'disorders/differences of sexual development/differentiation'.

synonyms.text: male with differences of sexual differentiation
male with disorder of sexual development

annotations.label: diandrous
annotations.definition: A phenotypic sex quality inhering in a  plant organism or a population of plants in which the flowers have two stamens.

annotations.label: monandrous
annotations.definition: A phenotypic sex quality inhering in a  plant organism or a population of plants in which the flowers have only one stamen.

annotations.label: protogynous
annotations.definition: A sequential hermaphroditic quality whereby organisms start out as female and change to male at a later life stage.

annotations.label: protandrous
annotations.definition: A sequential hermaphroditic quality whereby organisms start out as male and change to female at a later life stage.

annotations.label: protogynous hermaphroditism
annotations.definition: A protogynous quality whereby organisms start out as female and change to hermaphrodite at a later life stage.

annotations.label: protandrous hermaphroditism
annotations.definition: A protandrous quality whereby organisms start out as male and change to hermaphrodite at a later life stage.

annotations.label: female with DSD
annotations.definition: A biological sex quality inhering in an individual or a population who are otherwise female of a non-hermaphroditic species who has ambiguous or atypical congenital development of the reproductive system.
annotations.comment: The term intersex is popular in use, but may be considered offensive when applied to humans. DSD can be expanded multiple ways to 'disorders/differences of sexual development/differentiation'.

synonyms.text: female with differences of sexual differentiation
female with disorder of sexual development

annotations.label: population process quality
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a process by virtue of that process having a population or part of a population, where population is defined as a collection of individuals from the same taxonomic class distinguished by one or more characteristics, as participants.

annotations.label: absence of physical object
annotations.definition: A quality denoting the lack of a physical entity or object, such as a chemical.

annotations.label: absent process
annotations.definition: A quality denoting the lack of a process or function.

annotations.label: absence of anatomical entity
annotations.definition: A quality denoting the lack of an anatomical entity or cellular component.
annotations.comment: Absence of anatomical entity should only be used when all of the entity is absent. For example, absent teeth would mean all teeth are absent not some teeth are absent.

annotations.label: thermoplastic
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's disposition to deform by heating.

annotations.label: nuclear/cytoplasmic ratio

annotations.label: high nuclear/cytoplasmic ratio

annotations.comment: A high nuclear/cytolasmic ratio is 70% or higher.

annotations.label: cardinal direction
annotations.definition: The four cardinal directions or cardinal points are the directions north, east, south, and west, commonly denoted by their initials, N, E, S, W. East and west are at right angles to north and south, with east being in the clockwise direction of rotation from north and west being directly opposite east.

annotations.label: north
annotations.definition: North is one of the four compass points or cardinal directions. It is the opposite of south and is perpendicular to east and west.

annotations.label: east
annotations.definition: East is one of the four cardinal directions or points of the compass. It is the opposite direction from west.

annotations.label: south
annotations.definition: South is one of the four cardinal directions or compass points. South is the polar opposite of north and is perpendicular to east and west.

annotations.label: west
annotations.definition: West is one of the four cardinal directions or points of the compass. It is the opposite direction from east.

annotations.label: rate of occurence
annotations.definition: The number of repeated events per unit time, occurring in a repeating series. (e.g. the number of heart beats occurring over 1 minute)

annotations.label: rate of continuous process
annotations.definition: The rate of output of a continuous process. Example include: growth rate, cell migration rate, enzyme reaction rate.

annotations.label: decreased spatial extent of a process
annotations.definition: A spatial distribution of a process in which the process occupies a smaller length, area, or volume than the reference process. For example, heterochromatin may form over a shorter length of a chromosome in a mutant than in wild type.

annotations.label: increased spatial extent of a process
annotations.definition: A spatial distribution of a process in which the process occupies a greater length, area, or volume than the reference process. For example, heterochromatin may form over a shorter length of a chromosome in a mutant than in wild type.

annotations.label: increased rate of occurrence
annotations.definition: A rate of occurrence that is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased rate of occurrence
annotations.definition: A rate of occurrence that is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased rate of continuous process
annotations.definition: A rate of output of a continuous process that is relatively high.

annotations.label: decreased rate of continuous process
annotations.definition: A rate of output of a continuous process that is relatively low.

annotations.label: sex-specific
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being present in one sex and absent from the other in members of the same species.

annotations.label: male-specific
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being present in males and absent from females in members of the same species.

annotations.label: female-specific
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being present in females and absent from males in members of the same species.

annotations.label: lysed
annotations.definition: The breakdown, disintegration, destruction of an entity due to the breaking of cell membranes by any means.

annotations.label: patchy pigmentation
annotations.definition: A degree of pigmentation quality that is diversified with patches, or irregularity.

annotations.label: sequential hermaphrodite
annotations.definition: A hermaphroditic quality that inheres in a population or organism where an organism changes is a different sexes during different life stages.
annotations.comment: Sequential hermaphrodity is generally characterized by an organism producing female gametes and male gametes during different life stages.

annotations.label: neurogliaform morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have a small round soma, a large number of short, smooth, or slightly beaded primary dendrites that give rise to only a few secondary branches, and a branched axon that establishes a dense axonal mesh with thin shafts.

annotations.label: basket cell morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in multipolar neurons with densely branched terminal axonal arborizations that form basket-like structures surrounding and synapsing to the somas of target cells.

annotations.label: small basket cell morphology
annotations.definition: A basket cell morphology that inheres in neurons with axonal arbors composed of frequent, short, curvy axonal branches that tend to be near their somata and within the same layer.

annotations.label: large basket cell morphology
annotations.definition: A basket cell morphology that inheres in neurons that are large, and typically ascend to give rise to many long horizontally and vertically projecting axon collaterals that traverse neighboring columns and can extend through all cortical layers.

annotations.label: nest basket cell morphology
annotations.definition: A basket cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have simpler dendritic arbors (compared to small or large basket cell morphology), and an axonal plexus of intermediate density, composed of a few long, smooth axonal branches.

annotations.label: cortical bipolar morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have two principal dendrites that emerge from the soma and begin to branch some distance from it, have few spines, and branch in narrow fields. This is in contrast to bitufted morphology where branching occurs close to the soma.
annotations.comment: This is specific to cortical bipolar cells and are distinguished from retinal bipolar cell morphology which has an axon and dendrite emerging from opposite ends of the soma.

annotations.label: Martinotti morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which are large and non-spiny, with dendrites radiating in a multipolar or bitufted pattern, often favouring the descending side, and axons that ascend directly from the superficial side of the soma, and travel upward without branching.

annotations.label: T Martinotti morphology
annotations.definition: A Martinotti cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have axons that form a horizontal ramification, making it T-shaped.

annotations.label: fan Martinotti morphology
annotations.definition: A Martinotti cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have axons that form a fan-like plexus.

annotations.label: stellate morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons that have dendritic processes radiating from the cell body forming a star-like shape.

annotations.label: chandelier cell morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which are small and round, and make highly specialized synapses with the axon initial segments of pyramidal neurons, wrapping them with candle-shaped synaptic 'cartridges'.
annotations.comment: Chandelier cells can be bitufted or multipolar. {xref="DOI:10.1016/B978-0-12-369497-3.10004-4"}

annotations.label: bitufted cell morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have two clusters of primary dendrites that originate directly and extend from opposite ends of the soma, and axons that form a plexus which spreads widely. Compared to the narrow vertical axonal projection of bipolar morphology, and the 'horse-tail' axonal cluster of double boquet cells, bitufted morphology has wide horizontal axonal spans, and branching that occur close to the soma. Their vertical projection is also less extensive and crosses mostly to neighboring layers.

annotations.label: spiny stellate cell morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have spiny basal dendrites and resemble pyramidal cells but lose their apical dendrites as they mature. Their axons nearly always emerge from the descending side of the soma, but often recurve and ascend with arc-like collaterals.

annotations.label: pyramidal family morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have a pyramidal shaped soma, a single axon, a large apical dendrite and multiple basal dendrites.

annotations.label: horizontal pyramidal morphology
annotations.definition: A pyramidal family morphology that inheres in neurons which have an apical tree which is oriented parallel to the pia.

annotations.label: standard pyramidal morphology
annotations.definition: A pyramidal family morphology that inheres in neurons which have a standard pyramidal shape with its apical dendrite aligned vertically.

annotations.label: tufted pyramidal morphology
annotations.definition: A pyramidal cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have a distinct tuft formation, distal from the soma.

annotations.label: untufted pyramidal morphology
annotations.definition: A pyramidal cell morphology that inheres in neurons which lack a clear tuft formation but extend to large radial distances.

annotations.label: stellate pyramidal morphology
annotations.definition: A pyramidal cell morphology that inheres in neurons which lack a tuft formation but extend to small radial distances forming a star-like shape.

annotations.label: inverted pyramidal morphology
annotations.definition: A pyramidal cell morphology that inheres in neurons which have an apical tree which is oriented towards the white matter.

annotations.label: unipolar neuron morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons with only one process, a neurite, that extends from the cell body. The neurite then branches to form dendritic and axonal processes.

annotations.label: multipolar neuron morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons which possess three or more neurites, usually a single axon and many dendrites and dendritic branches.

annotations.label: bitufted dendrite cell morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons with dendrites that are bidirectional, emerging from opposite ends of the soma, with the two arbors extending in radial or tangential directions.
annotations.comment: This morphology type is distinct from bitufted cell morphology in that it refers to bitufted dendrite morphology rather than bitufted cell morphology.

annotations.label: extratelencephalic projecting
annotations.definition: A neuron projection quality that is a characteristic of neurons whose soma is located in the telencephalon and that project to structures outside the telencephalon

annotations.label: corticothalamic projecting
annotations.definition: A neuron projection quality that is a characteristic of neurons with soma in the cerebral cortex and projections to the thalamus.

annotations.label: near projecting
annotations.definition: A neuron projection quality that is a characteristic of neurons with projections that terminate locally rather than distantly.

annotations.label: corticomedulla projecting
annotations.definition: A neuron projection quality that is a characteristic of neurons that project to the medulla oblongata from other brain regions.

annotations.label: neuron projection quality
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in a neuron by virtue of the relative location of its soma and the regions to which it projects and makes synaptic contacts in.

annotations.label: intratelencephalic projecting
annotations.definition: A neuron projection quality that is a characteristic of neurons with both soma and projections entirely within the telencephalon.

annotations.label: dendrite quality
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a neuron's dendrites by virtue of the bearer's physical composition.

annotations.label: spiny dendrite quality
annotations.definition: A dendrite quality inhering in a neuron by virtue of the bearer's dendrite having spines.

annotations.label: tufted dendrite quality
annotations.definition: A dendrite quality inhering in a neuron by virtue of the bearer's dendrite having a terminal tuft.

annotations.label: aspiny dendrite quality
annotations.definition: A dendrite quality inhering in a neuron by virtue of the bearer's dendrite having few or no recognizable dendritic spines.

annotations.label: varicose dendrite quality
annotations.definition: A dendrite quality inhering in a neuron by virtue of the bearer's dendrite having numerous swellings or varicosities along their length.

annotations.label: retinal bipolar morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in retinal neurons which have two principal fibres, an axon and a dendrite, that emerge from opposite end the soma.
annotations.comment: This is distinct from cortical bipolar morphology which has two dendrites emerging from opposite ends of the soma, rather than one axon and one dendrite.

annotations.label: anatomical structure quality
annotations.definition: A quality of continuant that exist at the anatomical level of organisation and anything under it. This includes, but is not limited to, cells , tissues, and components.

annotations.label: anatomical histological quality
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in an anotomical structure by virtue of its capacity to be stained by specific histological dyes.

annotations.label: neutrophillic
annotations.definition: An affinity inhering in an anatomical structure by virtue of the bearer exhibiting a molecular interaction that stains and colors, pale-pink, with Wright-Giemsa stain.

annotations.label: polychromatophilic
annotations.definition: An affinity inhering in an anatomical structure by virtue of the bearer exhibiting a molecular interaction for both basic and acid stains under specific pH conditions.

annotations.label: rusty
annotations.definition: A texture quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being covered by iron oxide as a result of oxidation.

annotations.label: quality of interaction of a substance with electromagnetic radiation
annotations.definition: A quality of a substance on which or through which electromagnetic radiation impinges or traverses with respect to radiation. This substance quality changes the quality of the incident radiation with respect to any of: intensity, direction, scatter and wavelength.

annotations.label: double bouquet morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons with bitufted dendritic morphology but with the special feature of a tight fascicular axonal cylinder that resembles a 'horse tail'.

annotations.label: bistratified dendrite cell morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons with dendrites that stratify at two and only two locations.

annotations.label: monostratified dendrite cell morphology
annotations.definition: A cell morphology that inheres in neurons with dendrites that stratify at one and only one location.

annotations.label: dendritic field size
annotations.definition: A 3-D extent quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the spatial region that is defined by the maximal span of the bearer's dendritic arbor.

annotations.label: quality of a fluid
annotations.definition: A physical quality inhering in an entity exhibiting the physical charachteristics of a material that flows under applied force.

annotations.label: salinity
annotations.definition: An osmolarity quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the total amount of ionic salts dissolved in the bearer.

annotations.label: organismal area to mass ratio
annotations.definition: Quotient (or ratio) of surface area of an organism divided by the mass of the same organism

annotations.label: cursorial
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's morphological and physiological adaptation for running.

annotations.label: fossorial
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's morphological and physiological adaptation for digging and living underground.

annotations.label: arboreal
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's morphological and physiological adaptation for living in trees.

annotations.label: crepuscular
annotations.definition: A quality of a process that happens during dawn and dusk.

annotations.label: nocturnal
annotations.definition: A quality of a process that happens during the hours of darkness.

annotations.label: diurnal
annotations.definition: A quality of a process that happens during the hours of daylight.

annotations.label: migratory
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's morphological and physiological adaptation for participating in long-distance movement of conspecifics, usually on a seasonal basis.

annotations.label: anecic
annotations.definition: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to burrow more than 20 cm deep into the soil and make permanent, vertical burrows.

annotations.label: quantitative
annotations.definition: A quality of an entity that can be represented numerically, including anything that can be counted, measured, or given a numerical value.

annotations.label: total mass
annotations.definition:
A quantitative quality that represents the sum of all 'mass' qualities of the component parts that constitute a whole entity. The total mass quality inheres in the whole entity and reflects the aggregate measure of mass across all its components, such that changes in the mass of individual components may not necessarily correspond to equivalent changes in the total mass of the whole.

Example of usage: "total body mass" represents the sum of the masses of all tissues and organs in the body, while "liver mass" represents only the mass of the liver. An increase in liver mass does not necessarily mean an increase in total body mass, as the mass of other organs or tissues may decrease simultaneously.

annotations.label: increased total mass
annotations.definition: A total mass which is higher than normal or average.

annotations.label: decreased total mass
annotations.definition: A total mass which is lower than normal or average.

annotations.label: total amount
annotations.definition:
A quantitative quality that represents the sum of all 'amount' qualities of the component parts that constitute a whole entity. The total amount quality inheres in the whole entity and reflects the aggregate measure across all its components, such that changes in the amount of individual components may not necessarily correspond to equivalent changes in the total amount of the whole.

Example of usage: "blood total protein amount" represents the sum of the amounts of all protein types in blood, while "blood leptin amount" represents only the amount of leptin proteins. An increase in blood leptin amount does not necessarily mean an increase in blood total protein amount, as other protein levels may decrease simultaneously.

annotations.label: decreased total amount
annotations.definition: A total amount which is relatively low.

annotations.label: increased total amount
annotations.definition: A total amount which is relatively high.

annotations.label: is opposite of
annotations.definition: x is the opposite of y if there exists some distance metric M, and there exists no z such as M(x,z) <= M(x,y) or M(y,z) <= M(y,x).
